origins.name: Alien
origins.description: The character is not human and probably not from Earth or at least Earth-616. The Marvel Multiverse features all sorts of different alien races. The most common of these—Kree, Skrulls and so on—have their own entries. If the character doesn't fit one of those, then this origin applies.

origins.name: Alien: Kree
origins.description: The character is a member of the Kree, a humanoid race from the planet Hala who formed the interstellar Kree Empire in the region of space known as the Large Magellanic Cloud. They resemble humans but are stronger and tougher. Many of them have blue skin, but a good number of them have pink skin. The air on Hala has much more nitrogen in it, and Kree who are raised in it (or something like it) have a hard time breathing the air on Earth.

origins.name: Alien: Shi'ar
origins.description: The character is a member of the Shi’ar, an avian humanoid race from the planet Chandilar who formed the interstellar Shi’ar Empire in the Shi'ar Galaxy. They resemble humans but are much stronger and have feathers instead of hair on their heads.

origins.name: Alien: Skrull
origins.description: The character is a member of the Skrull, a reptilian humanoid race of shape-shifters who formed the interstellar Skrull Empire. In their natural form, they have green skin, red or green eyes, pointed ears and furrowed chins. They sprang from the planet Skrullos in the Andromeda Galaxy. The original Skrulls could not shape-shift, but the Celestials experimented on them to form Eternal Skrulls and Deviant Skrulls, much like they did with humans on Earth. The Deviant Skrulls became the dominant form of the species, driving the originals to extinction.

origins.name: Atlantean
origins.description: The character is a amember Homo mermanus, a variant race of humanity. Atlanteans breathe water through gills on both sides of their neck, and they cannot breathe air for longer than ten minutes. If they live in the Atlantic Ocean, they likely have blue skin and hail from Atlantis. If they come from the Pacific Ocean, they likely have green skin and live in Lemuria.

origins.name: Deviant
origins.description: The character is a member of Homo descendus, a variant race of humanity created by the Celestials. They are scattered across Earth, but they mainly live in underground cities beneath the Pacific Ocean, close to Lemuria. Only one hundred Deviants were originally created, but many more have been born over the millennia since then. They vary a great deal physically, coming in all sizes, shapes, colors and textures. Each is born with a unique physical mutation, and they have the latent genetic ability to develop powers, much like mutants. Eternals are compelled to kill Deviant mutates as part of their principle “Correct excess deviation.”

origins.name: Eternal
origins.description: The character is amember of Homo immortalis, a variant race of humanity created by the Celestials. They are scattered across Earth, but their headquarters lies in the city of Olympia, high in the mountains in Greece and folded into an echo dimension. Some live on Titan (a moon of Saturn) instead. The Eternals live by three principles: >  Protect Celestials. > Protect the Machine (a sentient A.I. deeply connected with the Earth). >  Correct excess deviation. Only one hundred Eternals were originally created, and only two have been born since: Thanos and Eros. The character is either one of the lesser-known one hundred or a new Eternal brought to life through extraordinary means. Through a ritual usually initiated by the Prime Eternal (their official leader), Eternals can be called to be part of a collective being known as the Uni-Mind.

origins.name: High-Tech
origins.description: The character's powers arise from high-tech devices that are well beyond the limits of modern-day engineering. This can come in many forms.

origins.name: High-Tech: Android
origins.description: The character is an artificial person. Their body (when they have one) is made of plastic, metal and circuitry rather than flesh and blood. Their powers come from technology embedded in their artificial body.

origins.name: High-Tech: Battle Suit
origins.description: The character wears a high-tech suit that grants them powers. When the battle suit is removed, the character no longer has access to the powers it grants. They are entirely — or at least mostly — normal underneath. Most characters with this origin have the Extreme Appearance tag when wearing their battle suit.

origins.name: High-Tech: Cybernetics
origins.description: The character has high-tech, surgically embedded implants that grant them powers. These cannot be removed without great care or pain. If the implants are obvious, the character gets the Extreme Appearance tag. Otherwise, the implants have been hidden inside their body (as with the lacing of Wolverine’s skeleton with adamantium) and might be visible only when in use (like when Wolverine uses his claws), if then.

origins.name: High-Tech: Pym Particles
origins.description: The character uses Pym Particles (named after their inventor, Henry Pym) to dramatically grow or shrink in size. After long-enough exposure to Pym Particles, many people are able to generate such particles on their own without the need of external aid. They may still need to use extra Pym Particles to reach extremely large or small sizes.

origins.name: Inhuman
origins.description: The character is a member of Homo sapiens inhumanus, a variant race of humanity genetically engineered by the Kree. For millennia, they lived isolated from the world, but they emerged in recent years, led by Black Bolt. All Inhumans carry a latent gene that can be activated by the Terrigen Mist. The explosion of a Terrigen Bomb spread the mist across the entire planet, revealing that many humans (including Kamala Khan) had Inhuman genetics. Black Bolt was later forced to destroy the Inhumans, leaving few outside of the royal family alive. The character is one of those rare Inhumans who survived.

origins.name: Magic
origins.description: The character's powers arise from supernatural means. There are many different traditions within magic, each with their own teachings.

origins.name: Magic: Chaos Magic
origins.description: The character is able to use chaos magic, which allows them to bend reality to their will. It stems from the ancient god Chthon.

origins.name: Magic: Demonic
origins.description: The character taps into (or is cursed by) the powers of Hell. They may be a native of one of the Nine Circles of Hell, or their powers might simply come from a Hellish source.

origins.name: Magic: Sorcery
origins.description: The character's powers arise from the study and practice of sorcery.

origins.name: Monstrous
origins.description: The character is some kind of monster. They may have a human form — or may have once been human — but they get their powers from their unnatural monstrousness.

origins.name: Monstrous: Vampire
origins.description: A creature of the night, sustained by blood and darkness. Vampires gain supernatural power at the cost of severe limitations — they may only develop powers from their narrow allowed list.

origins.name: Monstrous: Werewolf
origins.description: The character is afflicted with lycanthropy, a magical disease that causes them to take on the shape of a werewolf during the three nights of the full moon. They can transmit this magical disease to victims of their choosing who survive their attacks. When a character becomes a werewolf, they gain the Lunar Transformation and Alternate Form tags. When the character takes on their werewolf form, they go up one rank and gain the rest of the listed tags, traits and powers. They do not gain any additional powers or traits from this bump in rank, but they do gain 5 ability points and add +1 to their damage multiplier. If they have the Heroic tag, they also add 1 to their Karma. When they return to human form, they lose the benefits of their werewolf form. The character can switch forms voluntarily at any point — except during the three nights that the moon is at its fullest. On those nights, while the moon is above the horizon, the character must transform into a werewolf and is ruled by their animal nature. They cannot help but hunt and attack any people or creatures they come across, no matter if they are friend or foe.

origins.name: Mutant
origins.description: The character’s powers spring from them being a member of the subspecies Homo superior. They have a latent X-Gene that grants them powers, often triggered at adolescence.

origins.name: Mutant: Artificial
origins.description: The character's powers spring from DNA manipulation — or a natural, inherent condition — that makes them appear to bea true mutant. They believe themselves to bea mutant, or they did for years. However, they were not born with an X-Gene.

origins.name: Mutant: External
origins.description: The character is a member of arare and powerful subspecies of immortal mutants that stretches back thousands of years. Roughly a thousand years ago, the Externals formed a group called the High Lords that continues to influence the world’s destiny from behind the scenes. Some of them believe Cannonball to be the latest of their kind, although this is unconfirmed.

origins.name: Mythic
origins.description: The character is a creature or person of myth and legend. While they may appear human, they are something else.

origins.name: Mythic: Asgardian
origins.description: The character is a member of the Asgardian people, resembling the Norse gods. They call Asgard their home. The character can be one of the many gods who have been named throughout history, or they can be one of the lesser-known gods of Asgard. They should choose something that they are the god of.

origins.name: Mythic: Olympian
origins.description: The character is a member of the Olympian people, resembling the gods of Ancient Greece and Rome. They call Olympus their home. The character can be one of the many gods who have been named throughout history, or they can be one of the lesser known gods of Olympus. They should choose something that they are the god of.

origins.name: Special Training
origins.description: A skilled individual who has honed their body and mind through rigorous discipline rather than superpowers. Their abilities are capped to human-achievable skills.

origins.name: Spirit Of Vengeance
origins.description: The character has been possessed by a Spirit of Vengeance. Their purpose is to find sinners and impose penance. When a character becomes possessed by a Spirit of Vengeance, they gain the Alternate Form tag, which they can use voluntarily at any point. When the character takes on their Ghost Rider form, they gain access to the listed tags, traits and powers. When they return to human form, they lose the benefits of their Ghost Rider form.

origins.name: Symbiote
origins.description: The character has bonded with one of the Klyntar, a race of alien symbiotes created by the alien god Knull. Most of these creatures on Earth display spiderlike powers. When a character bonds with a symbiote, they go up one rank and gain the listed tags, traits and powers. Any remaining powers they select are often from the SpiderPowers set but do not have to be. Many symbiotes have the Disguise power. If the symbiote is Venom or one of its progeny, Spider-Man (Peter Parker) cannot gain any benefits from his Spider-Sense power against the bonded character.

origins.name: Symbiote: Knull
origins.description: The character has bonded with one of the Klyntar, a race of alien symbiotes created by the alien god Knull and which has long been under his influence—unlike the Venom symbiote and its offspring. When a character bonds with such a symbiote, they go up one rank and gain the listed powers, which must be picked for the powers that the rank gives.

origins.name: Symbiote: Venom
origins.description: The character has bonded with a symbiote that is either the Venom symbiote or descended from it. When a character bonds with such a symbiote, they go up two ranks and gain the listed traits and powers, which must be picked for the powers that the ranks give.

origins.name: Unknown
origins.description: The character has developed powers but is not entirely sure how. It could be that the player or the Narrator knows, but the character does not, or maybe nobody else does either. Astonishing things happen all the time in the Marvel Multiverse, and there’s no way to classify them all. The source of these powers may be explained in time. It could turn out that the character actually has a standard type of origin, or it could be that they have an origin that's utterly unique.

origins.name: Weird Science
origins.description: The character is not human and probably not from Earth or at least Earth-616. The Marvel Multiverse features all sorts of different alien races. The most common of these—Kree, Skrulls and so on—have their own entries. If the character doesn't fit one of those, then this origin applies.

origins.name: Weird Science: Gamma Mutate
origins.description: The character has a genetic mutation that gave them powers once they were exposed to enough gamma radiation, transforming them physically. This often gives them a monstrous appearance and changes their coloration drastically, usually to green, although some become red or even blue. They can often transform back into their original form as well.

origins.name: Alien: Brood
origins.description: The character is a member of the Brood, one of the most feared alien species in the galaxy. The Brood are insectoid creatures overseen by Brood Queens who all serve the Empress Brood. The Empress rules the entire Brood population through a hive mind that extends across the universe. Only Brood Queens (including the Empress) canimplanta Brood embryo in creatures from other species, which is how the Brood reproduce. Queens prefer to implant embryos in an unconscious host to prevent the host from knowing about it. They often poison the host with their tail stinger and wait for them to fall before implanting the embryo, which neutralizes the poison. If a character is fully taken over by a Brood embryo implanted in them, they automatically gain this origin, along with the Extraordinary Origin trait.

origins.name: Alien: Marginalian
origins.description: A being from the fringes of known space. The Marginalian's unusual biology and innate dimensional sense emerge only with sufficient experience; minimum Rank 3 required.

origins.name: Alien: Technarch
origins.description: The character is a member of the alien species known as the Technarchy. These artificially intelligent techno-organic beings use a Transmode virus to infect other creatures and eventually take over a planet to prepare it to become part of the Phalanx or be devoured.

traits.name: Abrasive
traits.description: The character rubs people the wrong way. This gives them trouble when trying to make Ego checks to persuade someone to help them out. It gives them an edge when they're trying to make Ego checks to intimidate someone.

featureNote.ego: Trouble on Ego checks to persuade someone to help them out. | Edge on Ego checks to intimidate someone.

traits.name: Anathema
traits.description: The character suffers direct harm when exposed to [CUSTOM]. They cannot voluntarily enter the same space with [CUSTOM]. If they are touched by [CUSTOM], they instantly suffer the damage of a Rank 3 attack roll that automatically succeeds. This continues each turn until they are separated from [CUSTOM] or are killed or destroyed. Damage caused by [CUSTOM] ignores all damage reduction the character has and cannot be healed by their Healing Factor.

featureNote.health: If the character is touched by [CUSTOM], they instantly suffer the damage of a Rank 3 attack roll that automatically succeeds. This continues each turn until they are separated from [CUSTOM] or are killed or destroyed. Damage caused by Anathema ignores all damage reduction the character has and cannot be healed by their Healing Factor.

traits.name: Audience
traits.description: Many people follow the character's work and treat them with the respect they deserve. By making an Ego check, the character can persuade their audience to provide help in the form of information or resources. The Narrator determines the TN of the Ego check based on the favor requested.

featureNote.ego: The character may make an Ego check to persuade their audience to provide help in the form of information or resources. The Narrator determines the TN of the Ego check based on the favor requested.

traits.name: Battle Ready
traits.description: The character is always mentally prepared for any sort of trouble to start. Add +30 Focus.

traits.name: Beguiling
traits.description: The character has an edge when making an Ego check to persuade someone who could be attracted to them.

featureNote.ego: Edge on Ego checks to persuade someone who could be attracted to you

traits.name: Berserker
traits.description: The character often loses control of their temper. Any time they take physical damage, they must make an Ego check with a TN equal to the amount of damage done. If they fail, they go berserk and must charge at full speed into combat with the enemy who caused the damage. While berserk, the character has an edge on all close combat attacks but trouble on all other checks. The character remains berserk until the enemy who caused it is unconscious, or the character takes a turn to make an Ego check with the original TN to calm down.

featureNote.health: Any time the character takes physical damage, they must make an Ego check with a TN equal to the amount of damage done. If they fail, they go berserk and must charge at full speed into combat with the enemy who caused the damage. While berserk, the character has an edge on all close combat attacks but trouble on all other checks. The character remains berserk until the enemy who caused it is unconscious, or the character takes a turn to make an Ego check with the original TN to calm down.

traits.name: Big
traits.description: The character's size is big, which applies -1 to their Melee and Agility defenses, adds +1 to their Run Speed, and increases their reach to 2. They still occupy a single space.

traits.name: Bloodthirsty
traits.description: The character likes hurting people — even killing them. After they knock someone unconscious, they must make a Challenging Ego check to keep from continuing to attack them until they're dead. A character with this trait cannot take the Heroic tag.

traits.name: Breathe Different
traits.description: The character cannot breathe Earth's air for long. They lose 1 point of Health for every minute they do not have some sort of assistance to allow them to breathe properly.

traits.name: Clinician
traits.description: The character has an edge on Logic checks to determine what is medically wrong with someone they examine.

featureNote.logic: Edge on Logic checks to treat or heal others in a hospital, clinic or similar setting

traits.name: Clueless
traits.description: The character tends to shut out things they're not entirely focused on. They have trouble on any Vigilance checks to spot hidden or invisible things. Enemies have an edge on Agility checks to sneak near or past them.

featureNote.vigilance: Trouble on any Vigilance checks to spot hidden or invisible things

traits.name: Combat Expert
traits.description: The character knows how to handle themselves better than most. They have an edge on Melee attacks against enemies of Rank 1.

combatNote.melee: Edge on Melee attacks against enemies of Rank 1

traits.name: Combat Reflexes
traits.description: TThe character can react quickly in combat. This grants them one additional reaction each turn.

traits.name: Connections
traits.description: The character knows someone with access to and knowledge of a particular field. The connection could be a reporter, a police officer, a politician, a mobster and so on. By making an Ego check, the character can call on their contact to provide help in the form of clues, information or resources. The Narrator determines the TN of the Ego check based on the favor requested. This trait can be selected multiple times, using many different types. These include Celebrities, Community, Criminal, Espionage, Military, Outsiders, Police, Professional, Sources, Super Heroes and so on.

traits.name: Dealmaker
traits.description: The character is skilled at the art of negotiation. They have an edge on action checks that have to do with making deals.

featureNote.ego: Edge on checks that have to do with making deals

traits.name: Determination
traits.description: The character never gives up, even when they feel like they're at their worst. While demoralized, they do not gain trouble on all actions, though they still cannot maintain concentration or spend further Focus.

traits.name: Eidetic Memory
traits.description: The character rarely forgets anything. If their player forgets something, they can ask the Narrator to remind them.

traits.name: Enduring Constitution
traits.description: The character can function for up to 48 hours without sleep and has an edge on Resilience checks to overcome fatigue or weariness.

featureNote.resilience: edge on Resilience checks to overcome fatigue or weariness

traits.name: Enhanced Physique
traits.description: The character is stronger than regular humans. Treat them as one size bigger for lifting, carrying, swinging and throwing things. (This does not stack with other factors.)

traits.name: Extra Occupation
traits.description: The character has another occupation in addition to their first one. The player can choose any occupation they qualify for. They gain all the traits and tags associated with it.

traits.name: Extraordinary Origin
traits.description: The character has an additional origin. This trait can be selected multiple times, but each time must be cleared with the Narrator. Take care to make sure that the origins make some kind of sense when combined together.

traits.name: Famous
traits.description: The character is widely known — at least among a certain group of people or a population. They may be well-liked or they may be hated, but either way, they are famous. They have an edge when making an Ego check to persuade someone who thinks favorably of them. They have trouble when making an Ego check to persuade someone who dislikes them. If the character has a secret identity, this trait works for only one of their identities. However, it can be taken multiple times for multiple identities.

featureNote.ego: Edge on Ego checks to persuade someone who thinks favorably of them - Trouble on Ego check to persuade someone who dislikes them

traits.name: Fearless
traits.description: The character is extremely brave. They have an edge on any action checks required to deal with fear.

traits.name: First Aid
traits.description: The character knows how to administer first aid. They have an edge on Logic checks to stop bleeding.

featureNote.logic: Edge on Logic checks to stop bleeding

traits.name: Font of Information
traits.description: The character has an edge on Logic checks having to do with knowledge.

featureNote.logic: Edge on Logic checks to do with knowledge

traits.name: Free Running
traits.description: The character has an edge on Agility checks made to perform acrobatics during a movement action.

featureNote.agility: Edge on Agility checks to perform acrobatics during a movement action

traits.name: Fresh Eyes
traits.description: The character has their own way of doing things. This often makes them seem strange to those around them, but it means they can bring new perspectives to existing issues. They have an edge on Logic checks when faced with something for the first time.

featureNote.logic: Edge on Logic checks when faced with something for the first time

traits.name: Gearhead
traits.description: The character knows their way around machines. They have an edge on Logic checks to figure out how any machine works.

featureNote.logic: Edge on Logic checks to figure out how any machine works

traits.name: Glibness
traits.description: The character can strike up a conversation with anyone at any time and can often convince them to lend a hand. They have an edge on Ego checks to persuade characters they are speaking to for the first time.

featureNote.ego: Edge on Ego checks to persuade characters they are speaking to for the first time

traits.name: God Heritage
traits.description: The character - or at least one of their ancestors — is a god. Pick something that they are the god of. They don't have to be a god that most people have heard of. In fact, they can be the god of something entirely new. However, they cannot generally be the god of something that’s already been claimed by someone else in their pantheon. Other gods are often jealous of their positions and take issue with such rivals. The character has an edge when dealing with something that they are the god of.

traits.name: Gullible
traits.description: The character is easy to fool. People lying to them have an edge on their Ego checks to persuade the character of something.

featureNote.ego: Edge on Ego checks to persuade the character of something

traits.name: Hive Mind
traits.description: The character has some independence but is connected to others of their kind by a telepathic network with an unlimited range and an unlimited number of members. When given orders by the leader of that network, the character must make an Ego vs. TN 18 action check or follow those orders. On a Fantastic success, the character can choose to permanently break free from the network.

traits.name: Honest
traits.description: The character is a terrible liar. They have trouble any time they make an Ego check that involves telling a lie. However, they have an edge on any friendly Ego (persuasion) checks in which they're truthful.

featureNote.ego: Trouble any time they make an Ego check that involves telling a lie; Edge on any friendly Ego (persuasion) checks in which they're truthful

traits.name: Infector
traits.description: The character can infect a target with a disease, which must be chosen along with the trait. The most notable examples are the Carrion Virus and Spider-Wasp Venom.

customizable.placeholder: Brood implantation, Techno-Organic virus, Transmode virus, etc.

traits.name: Interrogation
traits.description: The character knows how to ask the right questions in the right way. They have an edge on Ego or Logic checks made when asking questions.

featureNote.ego: Edge on checks made when asking questions
featureNote.logic: Edge on checks made when asking questions

traits.name: Inventor
traits.description: The character is good at coming up with solutions on the fly. They have an edge on Logic checks when creating or repairing things.

featureNote.logic: Edge on Logic checks when creating or repairing things

traits.name: Investigation
traits.description: The character is an expert investigator. They have an edge on Vigilance checks to spot clues and on Logic checks related to interpreting clues. If they have access to a forensics lab at the time, they gain a second edge on such checks.

featureNote.vigilance: Edge on Vigilance checks to spot clues; if they have access to a forensics lab at the time, they gain a second edge on such checks
featureNote.logic: Edge on Logic checks related to interpreting clues; if they have access to a forensics lab at the time, they gain a second edge on such checks

traits.name: Iron Will
traits.description: Enemies have trouble on Ego attacks to control the character's mind or influence their behavior. Also, the character gains an edge on Ego checks to break free of mind control or other compulsions.

featureNote.ego: Edge on Ego checks to break free of mind control or other compulsions

traits.name: Legal Eagle
traits.description: The character knows the laws of their homeland and can help others navigate them. They have an edge on Logic checks when dealing with legalities.

featureNote.logic: Edge on Logic checks when dealing with legalities.

traits.name: Leverage
traits.description: The character is good at figuring out what people want and using it against them. They have an edge on Logic checks to investigate people and on Ego checks to persuade people they've investigated.

featureNote.ego: Edge on Ego checks to persuade people they've investigated
featureNote.logic: Edge on Logic checks to investigate people

traits.name: Loner
traits.description: The character does not play well with others. They cannot be given an edge via assistance by someone who is not a teammate.

traits.name: Magic Item Reliance
traits.description: The character relies on a magically empowered item for some or all of their powers. When they take Health damage that would render them unconscious, they can instead choose to lose all of their magic-item-related powers and remain conscious with 1 point of Health left. Similarly, if they take Focus damage that would render them demoralized, they can instead choose to lose all of their magic-item-related powers and have 1 point of Focus left. Magic items are notoriously difficult to destroy. They cannot be harmed by most attacks. However, they can be taken away or lost. Many characters would go to great lengths to recover the magic items that grant them powers.

traits.name: Monster
traits.description: The character is some kind of monster, often of a type spoken of in legend. They have an edge whenever they attempt to intimidate someone.

featureNote.ego: Edge on Ego checks made to intimidate someone

traits.name: Out of Shape
traits.description: The character is in poor physical condition. They are considered one size smaller for the purposes of lifting, carrying, swinging and throwing things.

traits.name: Piloting
traits.description: The character knows how to operate vehicles of all kinds. They have an edge on Agility checks triggered when piloting or driving a vehicle during a movement action. This applies to cars, boats, aircraft and so on.

featureNote.agility: Edge on Agility checks triggered when piloting or driving a vehicle during a movement action

traits.name: Presence
traits.description: The character knows how to command attention on demand. They have an edge on Ego checks that involve getting people to pay attention to them or to voluntarily do things for them.

featureNote.ego: Edge on Ego checks that involve getting people to pay attention to them or to voluntarily do things for them

traits.name: Public Speaking
traits.description: The character knows how to get the attention of a crowd, whether that’s at a political rally or in front of a jury or a classroom. They have an edge on Ego checks when attempting to persuade groups.

featureNote.ego: Edge on Ego checks when attempting to persuade groups

traits.name: Pundit
traits.description: The character knows how to break news items and put them into context to craft a story around them. They have an edge on Ego or Logic rolls made when giving their opinion via media.

featureNote.ego: Edge on Ego checks made when giving their opinion via media
featureNote.logic: Edge on Logic checks made when giving their opinion via media

traits.name: Quick Learner
traits.description: If the character fails an action check, they gain an edge on the check if they try the same action again on their next turn.

traits.name: Scientific Expertise
traits.description: The character has extensive scientific training. They have an edge on Logic checks made when dealing with scientific research. If they have access to an appropriate lab at the time, they gain a second edge on such checks.

featureNote.logic: Edge on Logic checks made when dealing with scientific research; if they have access to an appropriate lab at the time, they gain a second edge on such checks

traits.name: Signature Attack
traits.description: The character is known for favoring a particular kind of weapon or attack power. They have an edge when making attacks that way. This trait can be taken more than once, but each time must be with a different kind of weapon or attack power.

traits.name: Situational Awareness
traits.description: The character is trained to always keep an eye out for trouble. They have an edge on initiative checks.

traits.name: Skeptical
traits.description: The character is hard to fool. People lying to them have trouble on their Ego checks to persuade the character of something.

traits.name: Small
traits.description: The character's size is small, which adds +1 to their Melee and Agility defenses and takes -1 from their Run Speed.

traits.name: Sneaky
traits.description: The character has an edge on Agility checks when sneaking around. Enemies have trouble on Vigilance checks to detect the character when the character is invisible or hiding.

traits.name: Stranger
traits.description: The character doesn't understand local customs. They have trouble on checks made when trying to decipher such things or when trying to pass themselves off as a local.

featureNote.ego: Trouble on checks made when trying to decipher local customs or when trying to pass themselves off as a local
featureNote.logic: Trouble on checks made when trying to decipher local customs or when trying to pass themselves off as a local

traits.name: Surprising Power
traits.description: The character can choose a power they normally wouldn't be able to use. The character will still need to have any prerequisite powers, but they can ignore rank and origin requirements. This trait can be selected multiple times.

traits.name: Suspicious
traits.description: The character is naturally suspicious of people who are hiding something. When someone with a secret identity makes a Logic check to try to convince the character that they're mistaken about the secret identity, the character gives them trouble.

traits.name: Symbiotic Spider-Blinder
traits.description: While the character is bonded to a symbiote, they cannot be detected by the Spider-Sense of any character who was previously bonded to the same symbiote. This effect is passed on to the symbiote’s offspring as well. Spider-Sense detects the previously bonded symbiote as part of itself and thus not a threat. The affected character does not gain an edge on Vigilance checks to perceive danger from such a symbiote, nor do such symbiotes have trouble on Agility checks against the character's Vigilance defense.

traits.name: Tech Reliance
traits.description: The character relies on technology for powers. When they take damage that would render them unconscious, they can instead choose to lose all of their technology-related powers and remain conscious with 1 point of Health remaining. Assuming the character has access to parts and tools, lost powers can be repaired after a battle.

traits.name: Unusual Size
traits.description: The character's regular size is beyond those for small, average or big. The player must choose the character's size: microscopic, miniature, tiny, little, huge, gigantic, titanic or gargantuan. Apply the modifiers listed on the Resize Effects table permanently. Exception: Flight Speed is calculated as small-sized for smaller categories, and as big-sized for larger categories.

traits.name: Weakness
traits.description: The character suffers an unusual amount of harm from [CUSTOM]. Any attack made [CUSTOM] ignores all inherent damage reduction the character has and cannot be healed by their Healing Factor.

traits.name: Weird
traits.description: The character has something weird (or even wonderful) about them. This causes people to have strong reactions to them, both good and bad. They have an edge on all Ego checks to persuade people inclined to like them and trouble on all Ego checks against people inclined to dislike them.

featureNote.ego: Edge on all Ego checks to persuade people inclined to like them; Trouble on all Ego checks against people inclined to dislike them

tags.name: A.I.
tags.description: The character possesses an artificial intelligence in a computerized mind. Their digital nature means that copies or backups can be made of their mind and can even be transferred into other compatible forms.

tags.name: Alien Heritage
tags.description: The character—or at least one of their ancestors—is not from Earth. Pick an alien race that they belong to. If they were not raised on Earth, they should consider taking the Outsider occupation.

tags.name: Allspeak
tags.description: The character can communicate verbally with anyone else. They can understand any spoken language, and when they speak, everyone who hears them believes them to be speaking in the listener's native tongue.

tags.name: Alternate Form
tags.description: The character can change from their regular human form into another form and back. When in either form, the character can only access powers assigned to that form. In most cases, this means that the character can use powers only when in their alternate form. This tag applies to characters that switch from one humanoid form to another, like Colossus, Emma Frost and many of the Hulks. It also applies to characters that have inhuman forms, like werewolves and vampires.

tags.name: Amphibious
tags.description: The character can breathe just fine underwater and can see and hear well in it too.

tags.name: Auditory Issues
tags.description: The character has trouble hearing that can range from partial to full deafness.

tags.name: Authority
tags.description: The character has a legal authority of some kind. They might be a commissioned officer, privateer, bounty hunter, doctor, firearms dealer, lawyer, pharmacist, police officer, private investigator, mayor, governor and so on. This tag can be selected multiple times if appropriate.

tags.name: Backup
tags.description: The character is part of an organization—like a police force—that they can call for immediate backup at any time.

tags.name: Black Market Access
tags.description: The character knows how and where they can buy and sell hard-to-find and potentially illegal things.

tags.name: Chaotic
tags.description: The character has been affected by the chaos lord Chthon.

tags.name: Clone
tags.description: The character is a clone of another character.

tags.name: Convict
tags.description: The character has been convicted of a crime, which is reflected on their record.

tags.name: Cursed
tags.description: Willingly or not, the character is affiliated with Hell. This can open them up to all sorts of nefarious plots and plans.

tags.name: Deceased
tags.description: The character is technically dead—although presumably still able to interact with the rest of the world in some way. They might be a vampire, a ghost, a zombie or even a brain floating in a jar.

tags.name: Dependents
tags.description: The character has young children, elderly relatives or others who rely on them. They can't just abandon these people and must check on them and help care for them on a regular basis.

tags.name: Enemy
tags.description: There's someone out there who has sworn to defeat the character. The enemy is of at least the same rank as the character and is obsessed with bringing them down. This tag can be taken multiple times, but it should be reserved for truly obsessed foes.

tags.name: Eternally Immortal
tags.description: The character does not age and could theoretically live forever. If the character is killed, they can be brought back to life in the Activation Chamber found in the Exclusion, an Eternal base located beneath the South Pole. To make this happen, the Activation Chamber sacrifices the life of a random human anywhere on Earth. Restriction: For Eternals only.

tags.name: Extreme Appearance
tags.description: The character looks very different than the average person (wherever they happen to be) and cannot easily hide their identity. When this tag appears in origins in this chapter, it may give other useful information, like whether an extreme appearance is common.

tags.name: Green Door
tags.description: After being killed, the character can come back to life by passing through the Green Door, a portal that connects a part of the afterlife to Earth. This makes them effectively immortal. Restriction: For gamma mutates only.

tags.name: Headquarters
tags.description: The character has a base, cave or lair they can use to recover from wounds, repair or store equipment, work on inventions and so on. The location can be public or secret, and it can be as humble as a toolshed or as elaborate as an underground complex or a Manhattan skyscraper.

tags.name: Heroic
tags.description: The character acts heroically. They help people in need, and they do their best not to kill—even with people who might deserve it. Restriction: The character cannot also be Villainous.

tags.name: Hounded
tags.description: The character is a member of a group of people who are persecuted for simply being themselves.

tags.name: Hunted
tags.description: The character is hunted by the law for crimes they have supposedly (or actually) committed. If captured by the police, they may be arrested, tried for their crimes and potentially sent to jail.

tags.name: Imageless
tags.description: The character casts no reflection, and their image cannot be captured by cameras.

tags.name: Immunity
tags.description: The character is immune to the usual effects of [CUSTOM]. This can be selected multiple times.

tags.name: Inhuman Genes
tags.description: The character has latent Inhuman genes that cause them to develop super-powers when exposed to the Terrigen Mist. Restriction: For Inhumans only.

tags.name: Krakoan
tags.description: As a mutant, the character is automatically a citizen of the island-nation of Krakoa and can travel to and from there freely. Also, if they are killed and the Five—a group of powerful mutants—are able to work together, the character can be resurrected into an identical new body with most of their memories intact. If killed in Otherworld, the character can still be resurrected, but they will be changed in unpredictable ways. Just how this might affect the character is up to the Narrator. Restriction: For mutants only.

tags.name: Lab Access
tags.description: The character either owns or has access to a laboratory filled with the technical equipment, supplies and tools they need for their work. This can be anything from a well-stocked high-school classroom to a forensics lab to an industrial site.

tags.name: Linguist
tags.description: The character is fluent in a language other than their native tongue. This tag can be selected multiple times, once for each language.

tags.name: Lunar Transformation
tags.description: On the three nights that the moon is at its fullest and while the moon is above the horizon, the character must transform into a werewolf and is ruled by their animal nature. They cannot help but hunt and attack any people or creatures they come across, no matter if they are friend or foe. Restriction: For werewolves only.

tags.name: Mahd Wy'ry
tags.description: The character lives for so long that their accumulated memories have the potential to drive them insane. The only cure for this condition is to die and then be reborn with a restoration of their mind from a clean backup. This happens rarely, but the disease can progress quickly once it starts. Restriction: For Eternals only.

tags.name: Media Awareness
tags.description: The character is aware (or simply believes) that they are actually a character in some kind of consumable media, like a book, comic, movie, cartoon, video game or even a tabletop game. They understand the tropes of that medium and can comment on it and address the consumers of that media. However, they often keep such comments to themselves, as doing so makes them seem insane to those around them who do not have this tag.

tags.name: Mental Health Conditions
tags.description: The character has mental health conditions. These might include anxiety, depression, dissociative identity disorder or other conditions.

tags.name: Mentor
tags.description: The character knows an older person they can ask for advice, even when it comes to having super-powers. This tag can be selected multiple times.

tags.name: Mobility Issues
tags.description: The character has mobility issues. This can range from using a cane to using a wheelchair.

tags.name: Mute
tags.description: The character cannot speak. Unless otherwise specified, they can use other means to communicate.

tags.name: Mysterious
tags.description: The character does not entirely understand how or why they have powers. This can prove unsettling and perhaps even dangerous, or they can just take it in stride.

tags.name: Obligation
tags.description: The character is responsible to certain people, places or organizations who rely on them. This tag can be selected multiple times, using many different categories. These include family, school, society, sports, work and so on.

customizable.placeholder: ex. family, school, society, sports, work

tags.name: Patron
tags.description: The character has a wealthy benefactor they can ask to purchase them anything within reason. The patron could be an individual, an organization or even a government. The character's access to the resources the patron provides depends on their relationship with the patron. If they anger or embarrass their patron, they might be cut off and have to remove this tag. This is similar to the Rich tag, but the character is relying on someone else's wealth.

tags.name: Poor
tags.description: The character has little access to money and struggles to make ends meet. This can cause them all sorts of problems.

tags.name: Powerful
tags.description: The character has access to the resources of their entire community. In most cases, they must be careful about how they use such resources so that they dont lose that access.

tags.name: Public Identity
tags.description: The character's real identity is known to the public. This can make it easier for them to ask their friends and family—and even strangers—for help. Unfortunately, it also makes it easier for their enemies to find them and even to threaten those same friends and family.

tags.name: Radioactive
tags.description: The character constantly emits low levels of some sort of radiation. This can be picked up by a Geiger counter.

tags.name: Rich
tags.description: The character has access to plenty of money. If they want something normally and legally available to people, they can simply buy it.

tags.name: Secret Identity
tags.description: The character's real identity is a secret to the public, and they must take precautions to maintain this status. This might help keep the people they care about safe from their enemies, but it can also cause strain in those same relationships.

tags.name: Signature Item
tags.description: This is a broader form of the Signature Weapon tag. The character is known for owning and using a specific kind of item. This can be a weapon (like Hawkeye's bow or Elektra's sai), a vehicle (like the motorcycle of Ghost Rider [Johnny Blaze]) or anything else. This tag can be taken more than once, but each time must be with a different item.

tags.name: Signature Weapon
tags.description: The character is known for owning and using a specific type of weapon, like Hawkeye's bow or Elektra's sai. This tag can be taken more than once, but each time must be with a different weapon.

tags.name: Sorcerous
tags.description: The character has dedicated themself to the study of traditional magic, also known as sorcery.

tags.name: Streetwise
tags.description: The character knows how things are handled on the street, who's in charge of various criminal enterprises and how to avoid issues with them.

tags.name: Supernatural
tags.description: The character has powers that are magical or based on something magical.

tags.name: Villainous
tags.description: The character is a villain, someone who cares little for the rights of others, up to and including their right to live. Restriction: The character cannot also be Heroic.

tags.name: Vision Issues
tags.description: The character has trouble seeing that can range from partial to full blindness. This can also include color blindness, astigmatisms and being nearsighted or farsighted.

tags.name: Worshipped
tags.description: The character is worshipped by others as a god.

tags.name: Worthy
tags.description: The character is deemed worthy of using a particular iconic item. The criteria for being worthy of a particular item is imbued in it by its creator, and it's always up to the Narrator to determine if a character is actually worthy at any given moment. When taking this tag, the character should also name the iconic item to which it applies. For example, Worthy (Mjolnir).

tags.name: X-Gene
tags.description: The character has mutant genes that cause them to develop super-powers. Restriction: For mutants only.

tags.name: Young
tags.description: The character is a minor, under 18 years old. If possible, they must report in to their parents or guardians on a regular basis.

powers.name: Accuracy 1
powers.summary: +1 to Agility damage multiplier and Agility checks (non-attack).
powers.description: The character adds +1 to their Agility damage multiplier, and they gain a +1 bonus to Agility checks other than attacks.
powers.flavor: The character is an ace with ranged attacks.

powers.name: Accuracy 2
powers.summary: +2 to Agility damage multiplier and Agility checks (non-attack).
powers.description: he character adds +2 to their Agility damage multiplier, and they gain a +2 bonus to Agility checks other than attacks.
powers.flavor: The character is a sharpshooter.

powers.name: Accuracy 3
powers.summary: +3 to Agility damage multiplier and Agility checks (non-attack).
powers.description: The character adds +3 to their Agility damage multiplier, and they gain a +3 bonus to Agility checks other than attacks.
powers.flavor: The character could hit a fly at one hundred paces.

powers.name: Accuracy 4
powers.summary: +4 to Agility damage multiplier and Agility checks (non-attack).
powers.description: The character adds +4 to their Agility damage multiplier, and they gain a +4 bonus to Agility checks other than attacks.
powers.flavor: The character can out-target almost anyone.

powers.name: Additional Limbs
powers.summary: +1 Edge to Melee and Agility checks with extra prehensile limbs.
powers.description: The character has an additional prehensile appendage (like a tail) or a symmetrical pair of them (like arms) that can be used to lift objects, use tools or otherwise take actions that normally require the use of a hand. This grants them an edge in Melee and Agility checks. A character can have as many additional limbs as they like—within reason—but they get no additional advantages for them.
powers.flavor: The character has extra limbs that can grab objects and attack foes.

powers.name: Advance
powers.description: Any allies in earshot can be affected, up to a number equal to the character's Ego defense. Each affected ally can immediately use a free movement action to move toward the enemy that triggered the reaction.
powers.summary: Allies in earshot use free movement toward enemy.
powers.flavor: The character can advance their allies on the battlefield.
powers.trigger: The character (or an ally in line of sight) stuns or defeats an enemy.

powers.name: Always Ready
powers.summary: +1 Martial Arts reaction
powers.description: The character gains one additional reaction per round, which can be used only to activate a Martial Arts power.
powers.flavor: The character is prepared to fight.

powers.name: Anger
powers.summary: Spend Focus to add massive Melee damage bonus (+15 per 10 Focus).
powers.description: The character becomes angry and uses that anger to increase their strength. For every 10 points of Focus they spend, they add +15 to their Melee damage bonus and lose -2 from their Logic — and subsequently their Logic defense. While using this power, they cannot use any other powers that require concentration.
powers.flavor: You won't like them when they’e angry.
powers.trigger: The character is harmed by an attack

powers.name: Animal Bond
powers.summary: The character can communicate telepathically with one animal of their choice
powers.description: The character can communicate telepathically with [CUSTOM]. If [CUSTOM] dies, you may switch to another animal. The communication can be verbal, visual or even more complex, such as imparting location information. There is no limit to the distance of the communication, as long as the character and [CUSTOM] are in the same dimension. Animals with a telepathic bond are generally willing to communicate, even if they aren't friendly. Bonded animals often do whatever the character asks, even if it might put them in danger.
powers.flavor: The character can communicate with an animal friend.

customizable.placeholder: A specific animal you must have befriended before

powers.name: Animal Communication
powers.summary: he character can communicate telepathically with one taxonomic order of animals
powers.description: The character can communicate telepathically with [CUSTOM]. They can call out to them as a group up to 500 spaces per rank away, and they can communicate specifically with ones they have met or at least seen. The communication with the animals can be verbal, visual or even more complex, such as imparting location information. There is no limit to the distance of the communication, as long as the character and the animals are in the same dimension. Friendly animals often do whatever the character asks, as long as it doesn't put them in danger—and they may be willing to risk even that for one they consider a friend. Hostile animals simply ignore all such requests—which make them a poor choice for communication.
powers.flavor: The character can communicate with a type of animal.

customizable.placeholder: A specific taxonomic order of animals

powers.name: Animated Illusion
powers.summary: Create moving visual hologram within 50 spaces.
powers.description: The character creates a visual-only illusion any where in line of sight, within 50 spaces. The illusion can be anything up to four sizes larger than the character, and it can move freely within its limits. The character breaks concentration if they move beyond 50 spaces from the illusion or lose line of sight to it.
powers.flavor: By manipulating visual light, the character creates a moving hologram.

powers.name: Astral Form
powers.summary: Project an intangible flying avatar into the Astral Plane.
powers.flavor: The character can enter the Astral Plane.
powers.description: The character can project an avatar into the Astral Plane, leaving their physical body in a deep trance in the real world, where it is vulnerable to attack. While in the Astral Plane, they can interact with other characters in the Astral Plane, but they are intangible, invisible and generally undetectable in the real world. However, they can sense things in the real world. If the character is Rank 4 or higher, they can also take on a transparent form that can be seen in the real world. While in the Astral Plane, the character can fly, and in combat, their Flight Speed is equal to their rank times their Run Speed. Outside of combat, they can move 10 times as fast

featureNote.speed: Outside of combat, your flight speed is ×3.

powers.name: Attack Stance
powers.summary: Double Melee ability bonus to damage.
powers.description: The character doubles their Melee ability bonus to damage.
powers.flavor: The character enters an aggressive attack stance, ready to strike in any direction.

powers.name: Banging Heads
powers.summary: Melee check vs two enemies; deals full damage and knocks prone on success.
powers.description: The character makes one Melee check against the Melee defense scores of two enemies within reach. If the attack fails against either foe, it fails entirely. If the attack is a success against both foes, each enemy takes full damage.
powers.flavor: The character takes two nearby enemies and slams their heads into each other.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, each enemy is also knocked prone.

powers.name: Battle Plan
powers.summary: Allies in earshot get edges on action checks
powers.description: The character inspires one or more allies of their choice in earshot, up to the character's Vigilance. Inspired allies gain an edge on all action checks until the start of the character's next turn.
powers.flavor: The character always has a plan.

powers.name: Blazing-Fast Fists
powers.summary: The character has an edge on all Melee attacks.
powers.description: The character has an edge on all Melee attacks.
powers.flavor: The character can attack superfast.

powers.name: Blink
powers.summary: The character teleports a short distance away.
powers.description: The character teleports into a clear space they can see or have been to, up to their rank in spaces away. If someone was about to attack them and they are now out of reach or line of sight, the attack automatically fails. If they are still within reach or line of sight, the attack has trouble instead.
powers.flavor: The character teleports a short distance away.
powers.trigger: The character (or an ally in line of sight) stuns or defeats an enemy.

powers.name: Blink Barrage
powers.summary: Teleports rapidly to give opponents trouble
powers.description: The character teleports several times in quick succession and winds up in a clear space they can see or have been to, up to their rank in spaces away. Any attacks have trouble against them for one round.
powers.flavor: The character teleports rapidly around an opponent.

powers.name: Blink Defense
powers.summary: The character teleports a short distance away to dodge a projectile
powers.description: The character pops away just as the attack reaches them and then pops back into the same space, leaving the projectile behind. The character makes an Ego check with an edge against the attacker's Agility check result. On a success, the projectile from the attack is teleported away, someplace safe.
powers.flavor: The character teleports a short distance away.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the attack is turned against the attacker, using the Ego check the character just made as the attack check.
powers.trigger: The character or an ally within reach is the subject of a ranged attack.

powers.name: Blur
powers.summary: Any attacks against the character have trouble.
powers.description: Any attacks against the character have trouble.
powers.flavor: The character moves like a blur!
powers.trigger: The character is attacked

powers.name: Body Sheet
powers.summary: Gain Health DR 3 and Glide Speed; cannot make attacks.
powers.flavor: The character can flatten themselves like a sheet.
powers.description: The character gains Health Damage Reduction 3, but they cannot make attacks. They gain a Glide Speed equal to double their Run Speed.
powers.trigger: The character falls or is the target of an attack.

powers.name: Body Sphere
powers.summary: Form protective sphere (+3 Health DR) or grab/envelop an attacker.
powers.flavor: The character can form their body into a protective sphere.
powers.description: The character forms their body into a sphere, protecting up to one ally per rank, squeezed into the same space. Those inside the sphere gain Health Damage Reduction 3 against outside attacks, but they cannot move on their own until the character releases them. Alternatively, if the attacker is within the character's reach, the character can make an Agility check against the target’s Agility defense. On a success, the character envelops the attacker, grabbing them.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the character closes the sphere enough to pin the attacker too.
powers.trigger: The character or an ally is the target of an attack.

powers.name: Bolts of Balthakk
powers.summary: Ego vs Agility attack; spend Focus for Agility damage bonus.
powers.flavor: The character fires enchanted bots of electricity from their fingers.
powers.description: The character makes an Ego check with an edge against the Agility defense of a target in their line of sight. For this attack, add +1 to the character's Agility damage bonus for every 2 points of Focus they spend. On a success, an affected target takes that total damage.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, an affected target takes double that total damage and is stunned for one round.

powers.name: Boost Powers
powers.summary: Double range/duration and +1 damage multiplier for ally powers.
powers.flavor: The character turbocharges all of another character's powers!
powers.description: The character picks one character within 5 spaces and boosts all of their powers. If the powers have ranges or effective areas or durations, these are doubled. If the powers affect a damage multiplier, add 1 to the effects. Any effects that normally happen with a Fantastic successes automatically happen on any success, not just a Fantastic one. If the target's powers have costs, the character must pay the highest of them or a minimum of 15 Focus.

powers.name: Borrow Senses
powers.summary: Access the full senses of a telepathically linked or bonded target.
powers.flavor: The character can use another person's senses.
powers.description: The character can use the full senses of someone or something with whom they have established a telepathic link or bond. While they do, they retain the use of their own senses too.

powers.name: Bounce Back
powers.summary: Negate fall damage, stand up, and jump up to Run Speed.
powers.flavor: The character bounces back from even the hardest falls.
powers.description: The fall doesn't damage the character. If they are prone, they stand up. If they'd like, they can also jump a number of spaces, up to their Run Speed.
powers.trigger: The character takes damage from a fall or is knocked prone.

powers.name: Brace for Impact
powers.summary: Spend Focus to ignore equal amount of Health damage taken.
powers.flavor: The character mentally toughens themselves for a fight.
powers.description: For every point of Focus spent, the character can ignore 1 point of Health damage dealt by the attack.
powers.trigger: An enemy deals physical damage to the character.

powers.name: Brain Drain
powers.summary: Ego vs Vigilance attack to drain Focus and heal self.
powers.flavor: The character consumes the energies of another.
powers.description: The character makes an Ego attack against the target's Vigilance. If it succeeds, the target takes regular Focus damage, and the character heals half that much Focus for themselves.
powers.trigger: The target is grabbed.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the character heals their full Focus damage instead.

powers.name: Brawling
powers.summary: Use Melee defense score against Agility attacks too.
powers.flavor: The character has the moves and is hard to hit.
powers.description: The character can use their Melee defense score against Agility attacks too.

featureNote.melee: You can use your Melee defense score against Agility attacks too.

powers.name: Brilliance 1
powers.summary: +1 to Logic damage multiplier and Logic checks (non-attack).
powers.description: The character adds +1 to their Logic damage multiplier, and they gain a +1 bonus to Logic checks other than attacks.
powers.flavor: The character puts their brain to work.

powers.name: Brilliance 2
powers.summary: +2 to Logic damage multiplier and Logic checks (non-attack).
powers.description: The character adds +2 to their Logic damage multiplier, and they gain a +2 bonus to Logic checks other than attacks.
powers.flavor: Everything makes more sense to the character.

powers.name: Brilliance 3
powers.summary: +3 to Logic damage multiplier and Logic checks (non-attack).
powers.description: The character adds +3 to their Logic damage multiplier, and they gain a +3 bonus to Logic checks other than attacks.
powers.flavor: Little can stop the character's mind.

powers.name: Brilliance 4
powers.summary: +4 to Logic damage multiplier and Logic checks (non-attack).
powers.description: The character adds +4 to their Logic damage multiplier, and they gain a +4 bonus to Logic checks other than attacks.
powers.flavor: The character's intelligence is unparalleled.

powers.name: Bump Power
powers.summary: Double range/duration and +1 dmg multiplier for one ally power.
powers.flavor: The character bumps up another character s power.
powers.description: The character picks one power from another character within 5 spaces and boosts it. If the power has ranges or effective areas or durations, these are doubled. If the power affects a damage multiplier, add 1 to the effect. Any effects that normally happen with a Fantastic success automatically happen on any success, not just a Fantastic one. If the power has a cost, the character must pay it as well, with a minimum of 5 Focus.

powers.name: Call Animal 1
powers.summary: The character can call a Rank 1 animal to their side.
powers.description: The character can call a Rank 1 animal to their side. They must be able to communicate with the animal, and the animal must be able to reach them. The number of rounds it takes the animal to reach the character is up to the Narrator. If the animal reaches the character during combat, it can enter combat on the following round. The called animals are friendly to the character and any of the character’s allies. Under normal circumstances, the animals remain near the character for an hour before wandering off.
powers.flavor: The character can call an animal to their side.

powers.name: Call Animal 2
powers.summary: The character can call a Rank 2 animal to their side or up to two Rank 1 animals.
powers.description: The character can call a Rank 2 animal to their side or up to two Rank 1 animals. They must be able to communicate with the animal, and the animal must be able to reach them. The number of rounds it takes the animal to reach the character is up to the Narrator. If the animal reaches the character during combat, it can enter combat on the following round. The called animals are friendly to the character and any of the character’s allies. Under normal circumstances, the animals remain near the character for an hour before wandering off.
powers.flavor: The character can beckon a larger animal to their side.

powers.name: Call Animal 3
powers.summary: The character can call a Rank 3 animal to their side or up to two Rank 2 animals or four Rank 1 animals.
powers.description: The character can call a Rank 3 animal to their side or up to two Rank 2 animals or four Rank 1 animals. They must be able to communicate with the animal, and the animal must be able to reach them. The number of rounds it takes the animal to reach the character is up to the Narrator. If the animal reaches the character during combat, it can enter combat on the following round. The called animals are friendly to the character and any of the character’s allies. Under normal circumstances, the animals remain near the character for an hour before wandering off.
powers.flavor: The character can summon a strong creature to their side.

powers.name: Catch Bullets
powers.summary: Agility check to nullify a physical ranged attack.
powers.description: The character makes an Agility check, using the attacker's attack result as the target number. If the character’s check succeeds, the attack is nullified.
powers.flavor: The character is fast enough to catch bullets—safely!
powers.trigger: A ranged attack using physical projectiles (arrows, bullets and so on) is made against the character—or a character within their character's reach.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the character gets their reaction back.

powers.name: Chain Strikes
powers.summary: Melee attack with edge; repeat attack on Fantastic success.
powers.description: The character makes a close attack with an edge. If the attack is a success, the enemy takes regular damage. On a Fantastic success, the character can also make an additional Chain Strike.
powers.flavor: The character unleashes a hail of strikes.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the character can also make an additional Chain Strike.

powers.name: Change of Plans
powers.summary: Reaction to give an ally an edge to negate trouble on a check.
powers.description: The ally gains an edge on that action check.
powers.flavor: The character thinks of a better way.
powers.trigger: An ally has trouble on an action check.

powers.name: Charmed Life
powers.summary: Gain an edge on one action check each round.
powers.description: The character gains an edge on any single action check each round. This does not stack with any other edges.
powers.flavor: The character seems like they were born lucky.

powers.name: Cloak
powers.summary: Hide from minds (20 spaces/rank); TN is Logic defense.
powers.description: The character uses a mirage to block their presence from the minds of people within 20 spaces per rank. Cameras (for instance) can still record them, but when people look in the character's direction, they see nothing there. The target number for anyone trying to detect the character is the character's Logic defense.
powers.flavor: The character makes people believe they're not there.

powers.name: Cloak Group
powers.summary: Hide self and allies from minds; TN is Logic defense.
powers.description: Using a mirage, the character blocks their presence — and the presence of up to one other person per rank, within 10 spaces—from the minds of people within 20 spaces per rank. Cameras (for instance) can still record them, but when people look in the characters’ direction, they see nothing there. The target number for anyone trying to detect the character or the others protected is the character’s Logic defense.
powers.flavor: The character mentally hides themselves and their friends.

powers.name: Clobber
powers.summary: Melee attack; double damage and prone on Fantastic success.
powers.description: The character makes a close attack against an enemy. If the attack is a success, the enemy takes regular damage.
powers.flavor: The character pummels their foe with enough force to knock them flat.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the enemy takes double damage and is knocked prone.

powers.name: Clone Moves
powers.summary: Copy target's Special Training powers via concentration.
powers.description: The character picks another character within 10 spaces and in their line of sight and duplicates all their powers that could be selected with the Special Training origin. They can now use those powers as if they were always theirs. If the copied powers have costs, the character must pay the highest of them, or a minimum of 5 Focus. When the character uses a copied power, they must pay any cost normally as well.
powers.flavor: The character duplicates another character's combat moves.

powers.name: Clone Powers
powers.summary: Duplicate all of a target's powers via concentration.
powers.description: The character picks another character within 10 spaces and duplicates all of their powers. They can now use those powers as if they were always theirs. If the target’s powers have costs, the character must pay the highest of them, or a minimum of 15 Focus. When the character uses a copied power, they must pay any cost normally as well.
powers.flavor: The character duplicates all of another character's powers.

powers.name: Coiling Crush
powers.summary: Paralyze target; spend movement each turn to deal crushing damage.
powers.description: The character makes a Melee attack. On a success, the enemy is paralyzed. During the character's subsequent turns, they can spend their movement action to make a Melee check against the enemy's Resilience. If this succeeds, they inflict regular Health damage. During the paralyzed enemy's turn, they can make a Melee check against the character’s Melee defense to break free.
powers.flavor: The character wraps around a target and crushes it.
powers.trigger: The character grabs an enemy.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the enemy also takes regular damage. If activated on subsequent turns, on a Fantastic success, they do double damage instead.

powers.name: Combat Support
powers.summary: Once per battle, an ally automatically rolls a 1 on their dMv.
powers.description: Once per battle, the character chooses an ally in earshot. If the ally makes an action check before the start of the character's next turn, the ally automatically rolls a 1 on their Marvel die, and that die cannot be affected by trouble.
powers.flavor: The character gives a friend a hand.

powers.name: Combat Trickery
powers.summary: Once per battle, roll a 1 on dMv against equal/higher rank targets.
powers.description: Once per battle, when the character is attacking targets of equal or higher rank, the character automatically rolls a 1 on their Marvel die, and that die cannot be affected by trouble. If the character is attacking multiple targets, all the targets must be of equal or higher rank.
powers.flavor: The character is a tricky one!
powers.trigger: The character makes an attack

powers.name: Command
powers.summary: Logic vs Logic check to force linked target to obey single order.
powers.description: The character gives an order to a target with whom they've established a Telepathic Link. The character makes a Logic check against the target's Logic defense. On a success, the target complies with the order.  The command must be something that can be completed in a single action. If it involves harming someone, the character has trouble on the check. If it would cause the target to harm themselves, the character has double trouble on the check.
powers.flavor: The character forces someone to follow an order.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the character gains an edge the next time they use this power against this same target.

powers.name: Command Animal
powers.summary: The character can issue orders to an animal.
powers.description: The character can give an order to an animal (or group of animals) that they called with Call Animal or with which they have an Animal Bond. The animal must be within earshot or have another means of communicating with the character. If the animal is the same or a higher rank than the character, the character must make an Ego check against the animal’s Ego defense. On a success, the animal complies with the order. On a Fantastic success, the character gains an edge the next time they use this power against this same target. The command must be something that can be completed in a single turn. If it involves harming An ally has trouble on an action check. If it would cause the animal to harm itself, the character has double trouble on the check.
powers.flavor: The character can issue orders to an animal.

powers.name: Control Fog
powers.summary: Create or dispel fog blocking sight beyond 10 spaces.
powers.description: The character creates a thick fog for up to 50 spaces per rank around them that blocks all line of sight beyond 10 spaces. They can also dispel any fog in a similar area.
powers.flavor: The character can create or dispel a thick fog.

powers.name: Control Weather 1
powers.summary: The character can make it rain.
powers.description: It starts to rain in an area up to 5 miles across times the character's rank, centered on the character. All ranges are cut in half. Movement is not affected.

powers.name: Control Weather 2
powers.summary: Halve ranges and speeds in a 5-mile/rank storm area.
powers.description: It starts to storm in an area up to 5 miles across times the character's rank, centered on the character. All ranges and all speeds are cut in half.
powers.flavor: The character can make it storm.

powers.name: Control Weather 3
powers.summary: Create thunderstorm; use Elemental Blast as a reaction.
powers.description: It starts to storm in an area up to 5 miles across times the character's rank, centered on the character. All ranges and all speeds are cut in half. If the character also has the Elemental Blast (Electricity) power, they can use it as a reaction when attacked while this power is active.
powers.flavor: The character creates a thunderstorm.

powers.name: Control Weather 4
powers.summary: Create hurricane; ranges/speeds cut to 1/4; no flying.
powers.description: It starts to storm in an area up to 5 miles across times the character's rank, centered on the character. All ranges and all speeds are cut to one quarter normal, and flying is impossible. If the character also has the Elemental Barrage (Electricity) power, they can use it as a reaction when attacked while this power is active.
powers.flavor: The character generates a hurricane.

powers.name: Copy Ability
powers.summary: Replace your ability score with that of a target within 10 spaces.
powers.description: The character picks one ability score of another character within 10 spaces and duplicates it. They now use that ability score in place of their own.
powers.flavor: The character duplicates another character's ability.

powers.name: Copy Power
powers.summary: Duplicate one power from a target within 10 spaces via concentration.
powers.description: The character picks one power a target within 10 spaces has and duplicates it. They can now use it as if it was always theirs. When the character uses such a power, they must pay any cost normally as well.
powers.flavor: The character duplicates another character's power.

powers.name: Copy Psyche
powers.summary: Copy, transmit, or delete minds via telepathic link (rank limit).
powers.description: The character can copy the mind of a person with whom they have established a telepathic link and store it in their own mind. This power can also be used to transmit a mind—either theirs or a stored copy of someone else’s—into the mind of another person with whom they've established a telepathic link. Additionally, it can be used to erase a copy of a mind from a person's brain. A character can hold up to one extra mind in their head for every rank the character has. The person whose mind was copied suffers no ill effects. The character can have conversations with the minds stored in their head, and the guest minds can try to influence the character's decisions and actions. If the character wants to know something that’s in a guest mind, they can use the Mind Reading power on it. However, they don’t have to first establish a telepathic link to do so. At the end of every day that a character has more than one mind in their head, they must make a Resilience vs. TN 10 action check. If they fail, they lose 5 Focus. This Focus cannot be regained until the character gets rid of any extra minds. It may take a while, but eventually, the character is sure to wind up demoralized until they rid themselves of any extra minds. If a character with extra minds in their head wants to remove an extra mind, they can make a Logic vs. the mind’s Logic defense action check. If they succeed, they permanently delete the copied mind from their brain. If they fail, they lose 5 Focus. When a character with extra minds in their head is demoralized, any mind inside them can attempt to take over their body. To do so, the mind must make a Logic vs. the character's Logic defense action check. Ona success, the mind gains control of the character's body. (If more than one mind tries this at once, the highest result prevails.) On a Fantastic success, the mind takes over the If a target does not want to have an extra mind placed in their head in the first place, the target must first be demoralized before the attempt is made.
