powers.summary: Logic check to read/write unknown written language.
powers.description: When the character encounters an unknown written language for the first time, they can make a Logic vs. TN 13 action check to puzzle it out. If they already know any other version of the language, they have an edge. On a success, they can read and write the language.
powers.flavor: The character can figure out what someone has written.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, they can permanently read and write it and never need to make a check for it again.

powers.name: Unflappable Poise
powers.summary: Close attacks against you have trouble; double trouble in Defense Stance.
powers.description: Any close attacks against the character have trouble. While they use Defense Stance, such attacks have double trouble.
powers.flavor: The character can dodge most close attacks.

featureNote.melee: Any close attacks against you have trouble. While in Defense Stance, such attacks have double trouble.

powers.name: Unrelenting Smash
powers.summary: Melee check to damage all within reach; spend Focus to repeat.
powers.description: The character makes a Melee check and compares it against the Melee defense of every enemy within their reach. Any enemy the attack succeeds against takes half regular damage. The character may then pay 15 more Focus to do the same thing again—before which they can move up to half their speed with anything left from their normal movement action. They can keep doing this until they run out of speed or Focus. Each target can be affected by this attack by this character only once per round.
powers.flavor: The character smashes everything in reach.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, those enemies take full regular damage and are knocked prone instead.

powers.name: Unstoppable Assault
powers.summary: Weapon Melee check to damage all within reach; spend Focus to repeat.
powers.description: The character makes a Melee check and compares that against the Melee defense of every enemy within their reach. Any enemy the attack succeeds against takes half regular damage. The character may then pay 15 more Focus to do the same thing again—before which they can move up to half their Speed with whatever is left from their normal movement action. They can keep doing this until they run out of Speed or Focus. Each target can only be affected by this attack by this character once per round.
powers.flavor: The character hews a path through their foes.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, those enemies take full regular damage instead and [MELEE_EFFECT].

powers.name: Untouchable Position
powers.summary: Reaction to give subsequent close attacks trouble for 1 round.
powers.description: If the character is attacked, all other close attacks against them have trouble until the start of their next turn.
powers.flavor: The character's enemies struggle to gang up on them.

featureNote.melee: If you are attacked, all other close attacks against you have trouble until the start of you next turn.

powers.name: Vapors of Valtorr
powers.summary: Create inky mist (25 spaces/rank); Ego vs Vigilance for damage.
powers.description: The character creates an inky mist for up to 25 spaces per rank around them that blocks all line of sight beyond 5 spaces. On later turns, the character can have the mist attacks one target at a time. Make an Ego check against the target's Vigilance defense. On a success, the attack does regular damage.
powers.flavor: The character weaves an inky mist that can attack those inside it.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, it does double damage and blinds the target for one turn.

powers.name: Venom Bash
powers.summary: Melee attack dealing Health damage; stuns on Fantastic.
powers.description: The character makes a bare-handed Melee attack. If the attack is a success, it does Health damage.
powers.flavor: The character charges up their fist for a bioelectric punch.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, it does double damage, and if the target takes at least 1 point of Health damage, they are also stunned for one round.

powers.name: Venom Blast
powers.summary: Ranged Agility attack for regular damage; stuns on Fantastic.
powers.description: The character makes a ranged attack. If the attack is a success, it does regular damage.
powers.flavor: The character unleashes a bioelectric shock.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the attack does double damage, and if the target suffers any actual damage, they are also stunned for one round.

powers.name: Venom Burst
powers.summary: Melee check to knock prone all foes within 5 spaces.
powers.description: The character pounds the ground, arcing bioelectricity all around. Make a single Melee check and compare it to the Melee defense scores of any foes within 5 spaces of the character. On a success, affected targets are knocked prone.
powers.flavor: The character lets loose a massive bioelectric charge.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, they also take half Health damage.

powers.name: Venom Sword
powers.summary: Create bioelectric sword (+2 Melee multiplier) via concentration.
powers.description: The character can create a sword from their own bioelectricity and wield it in combat. It can be used against any target within reach, and it adds +2 to the character’s Melee damage multiplier for attacks made with it.
powers.flavor: The character can create a sword of bioelectricity.
powers.trigger: The character or ally within reach is attacked
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the attack does double damage, and if the target suffers any actual damage, they are also stunned for one round.

powers.name: Vicious Attack
powers.summary: Melee attack for regular damage; double damage and effect on Fantastic.
powers.description: The character makes a close attack. If the attack is a success, the enemy takes regular damage.
powers.flavor: The character strikes a felling blow.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the enemy takes double damage and [MELEE_EFFECT].

powers.name: Wallcrawling
powers.summary: Climb at full Speed and never lose grip.
powers.description: The character can climb at a speed equal to their regular speed. Under normal conditions, they never lose their grip, no matter the angle of the surface.
powers.flavor: The character can climb like a spider.

powers.name: Weapons Blazing
powers.summary: Two ranged Agility attacks for half damage each.
powers.description: The character splits their attack to make two ranged attacks against separate targets (or they can focus a single attack on a single target). Make a single Agility check and compare it to the targets’ Agility defenses. On a success, the affected target takes half regular damage.
powers.flavor: The character blazes away at two foes at once.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the affected target takes full damage, and the character can make a bonus attack with this power against any available target, with the same effect.

powers.name: Weather Chill
powers.summary: Drop temperature to 0°F in 1-mile area via concentration.
powers.description: The temperature in an area roughly a mile across falls as low as O°F.
powers.flavor: The character cools things down.

powers.name: Weather Warm
powers.summary: Raise temperature to 100°F in 1-mile area via concentration.
powers.description: The temperature in an area roughly a mile across climbs as high as 100°F,
powers.flavor: The character heats things up.

powers.name: Webcasting
powers.summary: Ranged Agility attack to paralyze; pins on Fantastic.
powers.description: The character makes a ranged attack on an enemy. If the attack is a success, the web paralyzes the enemy. Breaking free from webbing requires a Melee check (target number 16).
powers.flavor: The character can snare a target in a web.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the enemy is also pinned.

powers.name: Webgliding
powers.summary: Gain Glide movement mode at 2x Run Speed.
powers.description: The character gains the Glide movement mode, with a speed equal to double their Run Speed.
powers.flavor: The character can glide through the air on their webbing.

powers.name: Webgrabbing
powers.summary: Ranged Agility attack to grab/paralyze.
powers.description: The character makes a ranged attack on an enemy. If the attack is a success, the character grabs the target with a web.
powers.flavor: The character snares a foe in a web.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the target can also be pinned.

powers.name: Webslinging
powers.summary: Gain Swingline movement mode at 3x Run Speed.
powers.description: The character pains the swingline movement mode, with a Speed equal to triple their Run Speed, The reach of their swingline is equal to their Swingline Speed.
powers.flavor: The character can swing through the city on their webs.

featureNote.speed: Reach of swingline is = to Swingline Speed.

powers.name: Webtrapping
powers.summary: Create 5 spaces of difficult terrain/web traps (rank × 15 min).
powers.description: The character fills up to 5 spaces with sticky webs, and the affected spaces are considered difficult terrain. Any creature that starts its turn in or moves into an affected space must make an Agility check (target number 16) or be paralyzed by the webbing. Breaking free from webbing requires a Melee check (target number 16). The webs automatically dissapate after the character's rank times 15 minutes.
powers.flavor: The character creates a giant web to trap those within it.

powers.name: Whirling Frenzy
powers.summary: Agility check dealing half damage to all within reach.
powers.description: The character assaults a foe with a tornado of whirling attacks. Make an Agility check against the Agility defense of every enemy within reach. Success deals half regular damage.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the enemy takes full damage and [MELEE_EFFECT].

powers.name: Winds of Watoomb
powers.summary: Halve foe movement (10 spaces/rank); end smoke/mist powers.
powers.description: The character conjures swirling winds that they control. All movement by a foe within 10 spaces per the character's rank is cut in half. The character can also use this spell to push away smoke, mist or fog. If such a thing was created by a power, the character makes an Ego check with an edge against the Ego defense of the opposing controller. On a success, the controller's power ends. On a Fantastic success, the controller is knocked prone. Additionally, the Winds of Watoomb can be used to reinforce the Shield of the Seraphim. The character can transfer any Health damage that gets through the Shield of the Seraphim’s protection to their Focus instead, leaving the Shield of the Seraphim intact.
powers.trigger: Damage gets through the Shield of the Seraphim
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the controller is knocked prone.

powers.name: Wisdom
powers.summary: Use Ego defense score against Logic attacks too.
powers.description: The character can use their Ego defense score against Logic attacks too.

featureNote.ego: You can use your Ego defense score against Logic attacks too.

powers.name: Wisecracker
powers.summary: Reaction Ego attack to deal regular Focus damage.
powers.description: The character cracks a joke at the enemy's expense. Make an Ego attack. On a success, it does regular Focus damage.
powers.fantasticEffect: On a Fantastic success, the damage is doubled, and the target is stunned for one round.
powers.trigger: The character succeeds in an attack on an enemy in earshot, or an enemy in earshot fails an attack on them.

powers.name: Wish You Luck
powers.summary: The character picks an ally in line of sight. That ally gains an edge on all action checks made while this power is in effect. There is no limit on the range between the character and the ally once the power is activated. The character must pay the Focus cost at the start of each of their subsequent turns of concentration to keep the power working.
powers.flavor: The character can make a friend lucky, even from across the room.
powers.description: The character picks an ally in line of sight. That ally gains an edge on all action checks made while this power is in effect. There is no limit on the range between the character and the ally once the power is activated. The character must pay the Focus cost at the start of each of their subsequent turns of concentration to keep the power working.

powers.name: Test Power — Everything
powers.description: Full description shown in tooltips and combat detail panel.
powers.summary: Short version shown in the power row.
powers.flavor: This is flavor text for the power.

featureNote.karma: Note shown under the Karma tracker
featureNote.speed: Note shown above the speed rows
featureNote.initiative: Note shown above the initiative value
featureNote.standard: Note shown in the Standard combat column
featureNote.movement: Note shown in the Movement combat column
featureNote.reaction: Note shown in the Reactions combat column
featureNote.health: Takes double damage from fire sources.
featureNote.focus: Regains 10 Focus at the start of each round.
featureNote.drHealth: DR applies to all damage types.
featureNote.drFocus: Focus DR only applies vs. psychic attacks.
featureNote.melee: Cannot use Melee attacks while Grabbed.
featureNote.agility: Agility checks are made at disadvantage underground.
featureNote.ego: Ego checks made to resist mind control get +5.
featureNote.logic: Logic checks for tech have +3 from built-in scanner.

stunts.name: Arrow Pin
stunts.description: If the character gets a Fantastic success with Stopping Power while firing arrows, they can pin the target to a nearby surface, rather than making another attack. Breaking free from being pinned requires a noncombat Melee vs. TN 16 action check.

stunts.name: Asgardian Pin
stunts.description: If the character gets a Fantastic success with Clobber while wielding an iconic weapon made of uru, witha restriction based on a quality (like Mjolnir or Tormod), they can pin the target instead of knocking them prone. This requires them to place the iconic weapon on top of the target and leave it there, pinning the target in place. If the target meets the iconic weapon's restriction, they can remove it. Otherwise, they are pinned until someone else removes it.

stunts.name: Cracking Skulls
stunts.description: If the character gets a Fantastic success with Banging Heads, they can stun both targets rather than knocking them prone.

stunts.name: Elemental Exploit
stunts.description: Instead of making an Agility attack with Elemental Blast, the character can use the power to make a Melee attack against a target within reach. Instead of using the spent Focus to add to their damage bonus, for every 5 points of Focus they spend, they can ignore 1 point of the target’s Health Damage Reduction.

stunts.name: Elemental Rico-Back
stunts.description: If the character gets a Fantastic success with Elemental Ricochet, they can attack the same target with an extra attack, rather than having to attack a different target.

stunts.name: Face Claw
stunts.description: If the character gets a Fantastic success with a Melee attack while using claws, they can blind the target for one round instead of causing them to bleed.

infections.name: Spider-Wasp Venom
infections.description: Once the character is infected, they grow wasplike mandibles around their mouth and antennae from their forehead, their teeth become long and ferocious and their fingers become tipped with claws. They also become connected to Shathra’s hive mind and are controlled by her.

iconicItemRestriction.name: Only works three times per day
Only works in or with certain dimensions

iconicItemPowers.name: Adamantium
iconicItemPowers.description: Weapons made with this amazing metal ignore 1 level of Health Damage Reduction, and they cannot be destroyed by normal means. Unless the user has the Healing Factor power, items made with this material must be unattached. If they lose the Healing Factor, they must make a Challenging Resilience check at the end of each week. If they fail it, they lose 1 point of Resilience. If they reach —4 Resilience, they die.

iconicItemPowers.name: Allspeak
iconicItemPowers.description: Allspeak: The wielder can understand any spoken language and can be understood by others in their own language as well.

iconicItemPowers.name: Anti-Metal
iconicItemPowers.description: Weapons made with this type of Antarctic vibranium from the Savage Land can ignore all Health Damage Reduction that comes from metal. This includes most shields and battle suits. Also, such items cannot be destroyed by normal means.

iconicItemPowers.name: Asgardian Transformation
iconicItemPowers.description: When tapped on the ground, the item can transform the user into a mortal form or an Asgardian form. (The item itself can also transform into an innocuous-looking form to suit the user's mortal form.) The user's Asgardian form gains all of the item's powers. If a non-Asgardian user is separated from the item for more than one minute, they lose the powers the item grants and return to mortal form.

iconicItemPowers.name: Bandaging
iconicItemPowers.description: The item instantly stops the wearer from bleeding.

iconicItemPowers.name: Constructable
iconicItemPowers.description: The user constructs the item using their own powers. They can regenerate the item at will, without using any actions. Such items cannot be used by others and cannot have any Unattached restrictions.

iconicItemPowers.name: Damage Switch
iconicItemPowers.description: The item can use its powers to inflict Focus damage instead of Health damage, or vice versa. The type of damage must be declared when the attack is made.

iconicItemPowers.name: Darkforce Attack
iconicItemPowers.description: Each round, the item can make a free Ego attack, doing Focus damage, against a target the item has transported to the Darkforce dimension.
iconicItemPowers.flavor: Free Action (only vs targets in Darkforce dimension)

featureNote.standard: Darkforce Attack: Free Ego attack (Focus damage) against a target in the Darkforce dimension.

iconicItemPowers.name: Effect Options
iconicItemPowers.description: The item can have different elemental effects of the user's choice. If there are only two options, switching between the types costs 5 Focus. If there are more options, switching between types costs one action of any kind (movement, standard or reaction) and 5 Focus. Either way, the switch must be made before the attack.

iconicItemPowers.name: Glider
iconicItemPowers.description: Grants the user a Glide Speed equal to double their Run Speed. On its own, without a rider, the item has Flight 1.

iconicItemPowers.name: Godkiller
iconicItemPowers.description: The item ignores Damage Reduction effects due to Mythic origins.

iconicItemPowers.name: Mysterium
iconicItemPowers.description: This metal disrupts magic. If someone is touched with it, any ongoing magic-based effects they are using or affected by end immediately, including instant negation of any such damage reduction or protection. Any checks made for magic-based attacks targeted against someone in a mysterium battle suit or using a mysterium shield automatically fail. In addition, Spider-Sense cannot detect it.

iconicItemPowers.name: Neganite
iconicItemPowers.description: A rare material found in Dimension Z that can produce explosive blasts of kinetic energy. A weapon fashioned from it can either ignore 10 points of damage protection or add +1 Health Damage Multiplier. Unlike just about anything else, this actually does stack with other such bonuses. Switching between the two effects costs one action of any kind (movement, standard or reaction) and 5 Focus.

iconicItemPowers.name: Nuform
iconicItemPowers.description: A synthetic vibranium created by Roxxon. Weapons made with this amazing metal add +1 Health Damage Multiplier, and unlike just about anything else, this actually does stack with other such bonuses. However, if the item is not exposed to microwave radiation at least once per week, it transforms into anti-metal instead.

iconicItemPowers.name: Psychic Link
iconicItemPowers.description: The user can contact and control the item telepathically. If the item has senses, the user can tap into them. If the item is used remotely, it shares the user's turn in the initiative order. The user can spend their movement or standard actions to give the item either a movement action or a standard action (or, by spending both actions, both). The item can also use the user's reaction to react to things that happen to it. However, just like a regular character, the item can only use one standard action per turn.

featureNote.weapon: Psychic Link: Can contact and control the item telepathically. Item shares initiative. Spend movement/standard actions to give the item same. Can use your reaction.

iconicItemPowers.name: Ranged
iconicItemPowers.description: The item allows the user to use a power that normally requires a Melee check at range, requiring an Agility check instead.

iconicItemPowers.name: Reduced Focus
iconicItemPowers.description: Reduces the Focus cost for a particular power by -5.

customizable.placeholder: Select a power with a Focus cost...

iconicItemPowers.name: Returns When Thrown
iconicItemPowers.description: If thrown (and unrestrained), the item returns to the user at the start of the user's next turn.

iconicItemPowers.name: Reverbium
iconicItemPowers.description: This extremely rare artificial vibranium amplifies kinetic energy instead of absorbing it. Weapons made with this amazing metal add +1 Health Damage Multiplier, and unlike just about anything else, this actually does stack with other such bonuses. However, it is not nearly as durable as vibranium and can be destroyed by a gadget hacked to do so.

iconicItemPowers.name: Sacrifice
iconicItemPowers.description: Any Focus costs required to use the item's powers must be paid with Health instead.

iconicItemPowers.name: Salvation
iconicItemPowers.description: If the user is knocked unconscious, then during what would be the user's next turn (or turns), the item automatically uses its powers to remove the user from danger, if it can.

featureNote.health: Salvation: If knocked unconscious, the item automatically uses its powers to remove you from danger on your next turn.

iconicItemPowers.name: Stackable
iconicItemPowers.description: One of the item's numbered powers (like Mighty, Sturdy and so on) can stack +1 on top of the same power if the user already has it. For instance, if the item grants +1 Mighty and the user does not have the power, they gain Mighty 1. If the user has Mighty 2, using the item bumps them up to Mighty 3. This cannot go beyond the normal limit for such a power, so a character who already has Mighty 4 cannot gain this benefit.

customizable.placeholder: Select a numbered Standard Power from this item...

iconicItemPowers.name: Summonable
iconicItemPowers.description: The item can be mentally summoned to the user from any distance, as long as it is in the same dimension. Alternatively, the item can be summoned from another dimension directly to the user. This requires a standard action or reaction.

iconicItemPowers.name: Swingline
iconicItemPowers.description: The character gains the swingline movement mode, with a Swingline Speed equal to triple their Run Speed. The reach of their swingline is equal to their Swingline Speed, as is the range if the item is a ranged weapon.

featureNote.speed: Reach of swingline is = to Swingline Speed.

iconicItemPowers.name: Toolkit
iconicItemPowers.description: The item can contain a single hacked gadget at a time, and its owner has an edge when hacking a gadget for the item.

iconicItemPowers.name: Unkillable
iconicItemPowers.description: As long as the item is not destroyed, the user cannot be permanently killed. Their Health can be reduced to any negative amount, even far beyond the point of death, but they heal normally despite this.

iconicItemPowers.name: Unmovable
iconicItemPowers.description: The item can be moved only by those who are able to use it.

iconicItemPowers.name: Uru
iconicItemPowers.description: Weapons made with this amazing substance have unlimited range and usually have the Mythic: Asgardian origin. If the item has a restriction related to a quality the user must have (like requiring the Worthy tag or being wise) to wield it, it cannot be lifted by someone without the quality.

iconicItemPowers.name: Vibranium
iconicItemPowers.description: Weapons made with this amazing metal add +1 Health Damage Multiplier, and they cannot be destroyed by normal means. Unlike just about anything else, this actually does stack with other such bonuses. The vast majority of vibranium comes from Wakanda.

iconicItemPowers.name: Weapon
iconicItemPowers.description: The item is a weapon, and the entry gives the weapon's details, including its damage multiplier and range. If there are two entries separated by a slash under Damage Multiplier, the first is for Melee attacks, and the second is for Agility attacks. If there are two entries separated by a slash under Range, the first is the weapon's range for Melee attacks, and the second is the weapon's range for Agility attacks.

iconicItemPowers.name: Custom Item Description
iconicItemPowers.description: Adds a custom descriptive text to the item's main entry.

iconicItemPowers.name: Custom Weapon Description
iconicItemPowers.description: Adds a custom descriptive text to the item's weapon combat action. Requires the Weapon power.

iconicItems.name: Absorbing Man's Ball & Chain
restrictions: Use: Must use the same effect options as the Absorbing Man.
iconicItems.description: Crusher Creel was in prison, wearing a ball and chain, when he was transformed into the Absorbing Man. When he changes into a new material, so do they.

iconicItems.name: Adamantium Claws
iconicItems.description: A set of retractable claws embedded in the user's limbs, fashioned from razor-sharp adamantium.

iconicItems.name: All-Black
iconicItems.description: A necrosword created by Knull from a symbiote and improved with the power of the head of a Celestial he decapitated with it, which would become Knowhere.

iconicItems.name: Angel's Techno-Organic Wings
iconicItems.description: When in Archangel form, Angel can use these wings to fire feathery fléchettes (treat like a submachine gun), and he can wrap his wings around him for protection.
iconicItems.note: Angel already has Flight 2, so for him, the Power Value is 1.

iconicItems.name: Arkon's Lightning Bolts
iconicItems.description: Arkon carries bolts that transform into lightning as he hurls them.

iconicItems.name: AVENG.E.R.S. Jacket
iconicItems.description: A high-tech jacket worn by members of the Avengers Emergency Response Squad.

iconicItems.name: Betsy Braddock's Psychic Sword
iconicItems.description: A blade that Betsy Braddock (the current Captain Britain) can summon with the power of her mind. It can deal either Focus damage or Health damage with any success, as she chooses.

iconicItems.name: Black Panther's Claws
iconicItems.description: The claws in the Black Panther's gloves are made of anti-metal and are electrified.

iconicItems.name: Blink's Displacement-Field Daggers
iconicItems.description: Blink can fashion her teleporting powers into knives that can affect targets they hit.
iconicItems.note: Blink already has Teleport Object and Teleport Other, so for her the Power Value is 1.

iconicItems.name: Bloodaxe (Original)
iconicItems.description: A vicious ax forged in Asgard for the Executioner, it infects its user with its lust for blood.

iconicItems.name: Bloodaxe (New)
powers: Power Slider (Bloodied): If the user gets a Fantastic success on an attack with this weapon and causes bleeding, their powers are boosted. If they are knocked unconscious by a foe in battle, they die
iconicItems.description: A new Bloodaxe forged for the Executioner so that he might reclaim the righteous death he believes Thor Odinson stole from him.

iconicItems.name: Boomerang's Boomerangs
powers: Effect Options: Each boomerang the character throws can have a different elemental effect of their choice
iconicItems.description: Boomerang wears a collection of trick boomerangs, allowing him to deploy different ones at will.

iconicItems.name: Captain America's Shield
iconicItems.description: The iconic shield of Captain America (Steve Rogers) is fashioned from a unique alloy of steel, vibranium and proto-adamantium. It is shaped like a disc with three bands around a blue circle, inside of which is a five-pointed star. A similar one was forged for Sam Wilson.

iconicItems.name: Captain America of the Railways' Shield
iconicItems.description: Captain America of the Railways originally carried a rough-made shield, but while at Avengers Academy, Moon Girl forged him a new one that is nearly as amazing as that of the original Captain America.

iconicItems.name: Cloak's Cloak
iconicItems.description: Cloak's garment can transport a grabbed target to the Darkforce dimension, where they are at his mercy.

iconicItems.name: Corvus Glaive's Glaive
iconicItems.description: Corvus Glaive wields a special weapon that protects him as much as it harms others.

iconicItems.name: Crimson Gem of Cyttorak
iconicItems.description: When Cain Marko bonded with the Crimson Gem of Cyttorak, it transformed him into a living Juggernaut.

iconicItems.name: Crimson Gem-Powered Bands of Cyttorak
iconicItems.note: This functions much like the Crimson Gem of Cyttorak, but it also grants Uncanny 4, and the user can manifest the Juggernaut armor and its powers at will. To use it with his profile from the Core Rulebook, remove his Surprising Power trait.
iconicItems.description: When the Crimson Gem of Cyttorak was destroyed, Cain Marko bonded with a manifestation of the Crimson Bands of Cyttorak charged up with the remnants of the gem.

iconicItems.name: Dagger's Lightforce Daggers
iconicItems.description: Dagger can use her powers to create and throw knives made of light.

iconicItems.name: Daredevil's Billy Club
iconicItems.description: A weapon consisting of two clubs connected by a length of wire, this can be disguised as a folding cane for the blind.

iconicItems.name: Destroyer's Energy Baton
iconicItems.description: As the new Destroyer, Sharon Carter wields this crackling baton, forged from neganite from Dimension Z.

iconicItems.name: Hawkeye's Trick Quiver
powers: Effect Options: Each arrow the character shoots can have a different elemental effect of their choice
iconicItems.description: Hawkeye (Clint Barton) created this quiver to hold a handy supply of trick arrows. He later gave it to Kate Bishop.

iconicItems.name: Doctor Octopus' Ocktoid Tentacles
iconicItems.description: These semiautonomous drones have a psychic link with Octavius and can form four arms for him or can separate into individual pieces that work in concert with each other.

iconicItems.name: Hell-Mark
restrictions: Use: Dimensional Portal only goes to and from Hell.
iconicItems.description: Candidates to become a Hell Lord bear the mark of Hell on their skin.

iconicItems.name: Ebony Blade
powers: Power Slider (Blood Curse): If the user gets a Fantastic success on an attack with this weapon and causes bleeding, their powers are boosted. However, they also gain the Bloodthirsty tag. If they kill someone while Bloodthirsty, they lose their Heroic tag
restrictions: Use: Unkillable power usable only by descendants of King Arthur.
iconicItems.description: This legendary magical weapon was handed down through the generations and used by the Black Knight.

iconicItems.name: Gambit's Charged Cards
iconicItems.description: Gambit can charge up other things with his powers, but he favors using a deck of cards and carries one with him.

iconicItems.name: H.E.R.B.I.E.'s Opti-Blaster
iconicItems.description: A light-based blaster that Mister Fantastic built into his robotic helper.

iconicItems.name: Hofund
iconicItems.description: The sword of Heimdall, the guardian of Asgard's Rainbow Bridge, went to Sif after his death, along with the job.

iconicItems.name: Jarnbjorn
iconicItems.description: The battleax of the Valkyrie named Rúna. When she disappeared for centuries, Thor made use of it until he took up Mjolnir.

iconicItems.name: Juggernaut's Helmet
iconicItems.description: The iconic helmet of the Juggernaut, forged through the power of the Crimson Gem of Cyttorak.

iconicItems.name: Mjolnir
iconicItems.note: This adds the Summon Portal power, which did not appear with Thor's profile in the Core Rulebook.
iconicItems.description: "Whosoever holds this hammer, if they be worthy, shall possess the power of Thor."

iconicItems.name: Karnak's Hands
iconicItems.description: Karnak's hands can be used as lethal weapons.

iconicItems.name: Magik's Soulsword
restrictions: Use: Discipline power only works against magical creatures.
iconicItems.description: A blade forged from Illyana Rasputin's own soul, this also serves as her icon as the ruler of Limbo.

iconicItems.name: Magneto's Helmet
iconicItems.description: A high-tech helmet worn by Magneto that protects his mind and grants him psychic resilience.

iconicItems.name: Mjolnir (Earth-1610)
iconicItems.description: On Earth-1610, Mjolnir is a high-tech weapon that allows Thor full access to his Asgardian powers.

iconicItems.name: Nemesis' Scell
iconicItems.description: This soulbound sword grants its user powers in exchange for lives.

iconicItems.name: Night Nurse's Gauntlet
iconicItems.description: Iron Man gave Night Nurse a gauntlet from one of his battle suits to help her protect herself.

iconicItems.name: Paladin's Stun Gun
iconicItems.description: This special gun does no permanent damage and is locked so only Paladin can use it.

iconicItems.name: Patriot's Shield
iconicItems.description: A high-tech shield designed for Patriot. It has a psychic link with him, and he can use it to fire energy blasts.

iconicItems.name: Queen Goblin's Pumpkin Scepter
iconicItems.description: A double-headed mace that dispenses pain to both body and soul.

iconicItems.name: Ren Kimura's Razor Arms
iconicItems.description: Kimura's steely arms can slice like knives.

iconicItems.name: Shard of the Crimson Gem of Cyttorak
iconicItems.description: This piece of the original Crimson Gem of Cyttorak grants the user incredible powers, transforming them into a minor Juggernaut.

iconicItems.name: Proxima Midnight's Spear
iconicItems.description: A weapon forged for Proxima Midnight by Thanos himself.

iconicItems.name: Silver Surfer's Surfboard
iconicItems.description: A silver board on which Galactus' most famous herald rides through the cosmos.

iconicItems.name: Sin-Eater's Sin-Eating Shotgun
iconicItems.description: A shotgun that takes away its victims' sins.

iconicItems.name: Star-Lord's Element Gun
iconicItems.description: A pistol that can wield the power of the elements.

iconicItems.name: Thunderball's Ball & Chain
iconicItems.description: An enchanted ball and chain that Thunderball carries from his prison days.

iconicItems.name: Stormbreaker
restrictions: Can only be used by the worthy
iconicItems.note: This item is functionally identical to Mjolnir in all but shape.
iconicItems.description: After Beta Ray Bill gave Mjolnir back to Thor, Odin had a replacement crafted for the Korbinite.

iconicItems.name: Swordsman's Sword
iconicItems.description: The blade wielded by Swordsman, using technology from the alien Makluans.

iconicItems.name: Ten Rings
iconicItems.description: Ten bracelet-sized rings that use the magic of Ta-Lo to grant their user incredible powers.

iconicItems.name: Thunderstrike
restrictions: Can only be used by the worthy
iconicItems.note: This item is functionally identical to Mjolnir in all but shape. The Thunderstrike (Kevin Masterson) of Earth-982 gained his powers by magically merging with the mace of the same name, eliminating the iconic item in that universe.
iconicItems.description: After Eric Masterson lost Mjolnir to Thor, Odin forged a mace for Masterson. The inscription on the side of it reads, "The world still needs heroes. Thunderstrike." Eric's son, Kevin, inherited it after Eric's death.

iconicItems.name: Tormod
iconicItems.description: A weapon forged by Thor Odinson himself, from the metal of his helmet.

iconicItems.name: Twilight Sword
powers: Custom Weapon Desciption: On a Fantastic success, the Twilight Sword can count as a fire attack rather than a sharp attack
restrictions: Can only be used by the worthy
iconicItems.description: After Thor destroyed Stormbreaker, Beta Ray Bill claimed Surtur's old blade instead.

iconicItems.name: U.S.Agent's Shield
iconicItems.description: The black-white-and-red shield used by U.S.Agent. It originally belonged to the Captain America of Earth-13584, but it was given away after his death.

iconicItems.name: Valkyrie Spear
iconicItems.description: A traditional spear carried by the Valkyrior of Asgard.

iconicItems.name: Whiplash's Energy Whips
iconicItems.description: These charged whips extend from the arms of Whiplash's armor and lash out at his foes.

iconicItems.name: Widow's Bite
iconicItems.description: A wrist-worn weapon that fires stinging blasts.

iconicItems.name: Wrecker's Crowbar
iconicItems.description: An enchanted crowbar that Wrecker carries to help destroy anything in his path.

iconicItems.name: Zodiac Key
iconicItems.description: A weapon that resembles an ankh. The user holds it by its ring and points the tip of it at the target.

iconicItems.name: Spider-Arms
iconicItems.description: The gadget consists of a set of four long, spidery limbs attached to the character's back, which can be used in combat. This grants the user the Additional Limbs power with the Tech Reliance trait. The user gains the Extreme Appearance tag while the arms are deployed. The Spider-Arms can fold away to backpack size when not in use.

battleSuits.name: Black Widow's Battle Suit
battleSuits.description: Natasha Romanov of Earth-1610 wore a battle suit created by Tony Stark—right up till she betrayed him.

battleSuits.name: Doctor Doom's Armor
battleSuits.description: The only battle suit that befits Doom, fashioned with sorcery!

battleSuits.name: Hulkbuster Armor
battleSuits.description: Tony Stark's specialized armor designed for dealing with heavy hitters like the Hulk.

battleSuits.name: Iron Man's Armor (Earth-1610)
battleSuits.description: Tony Stark of Earth-1610 has his own way of making armor.

battleSuits.name: High Evolutionary's Armor
battleSuits.description: The High Evolutionary's genius extends not only to genetic manipulation but to the design of his own armor.

battleSuits.name: Iron Man's Standard Armor
battleSuits.description: The cutting-edge battle suit Tony Stark personally wears as Iron Man.

battleSuits.name: Iron Man's Model NIL Armor
battleSuits.description: The latest in stealth armor from the mind of Tony Stark.

battleSuits.name: Iron Man's Mysterium Armor
battleSuits.description: One of Tony Stark's greatest battle suits, designed to protect from the thing he hates most: magic.

battleSuits.name: Teen Immortus' Armor
battleSuits.description: The young version of Kang the Conqueror used this armor while traveling through time.

battleSuits.name: War Machine Armor
battleSuits.description: The battle suit Tony Stark built for his pal Rhodey.

battleSuits.name: Iron Spider Armor
battleSuits.description: The battle suit Tony Stark built for Spider-Man (Peter Parker).

battleSuits.name: Whiplash's Armor
battleSuits.description: A battle suit built out of an old set of Iron Man armor.

basicItems.name: Ball & Chain
basicItems.description: This is a chain attached to a heavy metal ball. regular users have a range of 5. Those with powers like Mighty can throw it farther.
basicItems.additionalDetails: This is a chain attached to a heavy metal ball. The other end is meant to be shackled to a prisoner's ankle, but those using it as a weapon are usually free, so they can not only swing it at targets but hurl it as well. It's considered Small size, so the range is 5 for regular users. Those with powers like Mighty can throw it farther

basicItems.name: Body Armor

featureNote.agility: Reduce Agility by -1 for one full turn after you use a movement action.

basicItems.description: Once damaged in combat, it becomes useless at the end Of that combat. This covers the wearer from head to toe. Unlike a battle suit, it is not powered. Because of its bulk, it reduces the wearer's Agility by —1 for one full turn after they use a movement action.

basicItems.name: Boomerang
basicItems.additionalDetails: Automatically returns to the user at the start of their next turn.

basicItems.name: Bow
basicItems.additionalDetails: The user can choose between blunt and sharp damage by using different arrows. They must declare which type they are using when they make the attack.

basicItems.name: Brass Knuckles
basicItems.additionalDetails: They cannot be thrown, but they give trouble to any attempt to disarm them from their user.

basicItems.name: Bulletproof Vest
basicItems.description: This covers the wearer's core, including most of their vital organs. Once damaged in combat, it becomes useless at the end of that combat.

basicItems.name: Chainsaw
basicItems.additionalDetails: This weapon has a noisy motor. It gives the user trouble when trying to hide or sneak.

basicItems.name: Claws
basicItems.additionalDetails: They cannot be thrown, but they give trouble to any attempt to disarm them from their user.

basicItems.name: Flamethrower
basicItems.additionalDetails: This weapon can attack up to three targets in adjacent spaces to which the attacker can draw a line of sight. Make a single attack roll and compare it to the Agility defense scores of the targets. Split the damage from that roll equally. On a Fantastic success, the targets are also set ablaze.

basicItems.name: Flash-Bang Grenade
basicItems.additionalDetails: This weapon affects all characters within 2 spaces of where it lands and to which the grenade (not its thrower) would have a line of sight past any intervening obstacles. Pick a target space and make a Challenging Agility check. If it succeeds, the grenade lands in the desired space. If the check fails, the grenade still winds up somewhere. Roll 1d6 to see how many spaces away from its intended target it lands. It’s up to the Narrator which direction it goes. Once you figure out where the grenade lands, compare the result of the check against the Agility defense of each character it could affect. Unlike with other, more precise weapons, a grenade uses its own damage multiplier (x2) instead of that of the attacker. On a Fantastic success, it does double that (in other words, x4) damage to the character in the space where it lands, and regular damage to all other affected targets. Once you figure out where the grenade lands, compare the attack roll against the Vigilance defense of any character within 2 spaces of it. Those who are affected are blinded and stunned for one round.

basicItems.name: Frag Grenade
basicItems.additionalDetails: This weapon affects all characters within 2 spaces of where it lands and to which the grenade (not its thrower) would have a line of sight past any intervening obstacles. Pick a target space and make a Challenging Agility check. If it succeeds, the grenade lands in the desired space. If the check fails, the grenade still winds up somewhere. Roll 1d6 to see how many spaces away from its intended target it lands. It’s up to the Narrator which direction it goes. Once you figure out where the grenade lands, compare the result of the check against the Agility defense of each character it could affect. Unlike with other, more precise weapons, a grenade uses its own damage multiplier (x2) instead of that of the attacker. On a Fantastic success, it does double that (in other words, x4) damage to the character in the space where it lands, and regular damage to all other affected targets.

basicItems.name: Glaive
basicItems.additionalDetails: Attempts to throw it have double trouble; it's melee range is increased by 1

basicItems.name: Grenade Launcher
basicItems.additionalDetails: This weapon can be outfitted with any type of grenade. It has double the normal range for thrown grenades, but the grenades otherwise work the same way.

basicItems.name: Night-Night Gun
basicItems.additionalDetails: This weapon fires tranquilizer darts. The user makes an Agility check against the target's Resilience defense. On a success, the target is stunned for one round, but the weapon does no other damage. On a Fantastic success, the target falls asleep instead.

basicItems.name: Rifle
basicItems.additionalDetails: Attacks with this weapon against targets 5 spaces away or fewer have trouble.

basicItems.name: Riot Shield
basicItems.description: If the user does not have the Shield 1 (or higher) power, they can only use this to protect themselves from attacks coming from a single 90-degree angle. They can only change this angle at the start of their turn. Once damaged in combat, it becomes useless at the end of that combat.

basicItems.name: Shotgun
basicItems.description: This weapon can attack up to two targets in adjacent spaces to which the attacker can draw a line of sight. Make a single attack roll and compare it to the Agility defense scores of both targets. Split the damage from that roll equally.

basicItems.name: Submachine Gun
basicItems.description: This weapon can attack up to three targets in adjacent spaces to which the attacker can draw a line of sight. Make a single attack roll and compare it to the Agility defense scores of the targets. Split the damage from that roll equally. Attacks with this weapon against targets 5 spaces away or fewer have trouble.

basicItems.name: Smoke Grenade
basicItems.additionalDetails: This weapon affects all characters within 2 spaces of where it lands and to which the grenade (not its thrower) would have a line of sight past any intervening obstacles. Pick a target space and make a Challenging Agility check. If it succeeds, the grenade lands in the desired space. If the check fails, the grenade still winds up somewhere. Roll 1d6 to see how many spaces away from its intended target it lands. It’s up to the Narrator which direction it goes. This grenade throws out harmless smoke that obscures vision. Anyone inside an affected space is considered blinded while they are in such a space. The smoke lasts for six rounds.

basicItems.name: Tear-Gas Grenade
basicItems.additionalDetails: This weapon affects all characters within 2 spaces of where it lands and to which the grenade (not its thrower) would have a line of sight past any intervening obstacles. Pick a target space and make a Challenging Agility check. If it succeeds, the grenade lands in the desired space. If the check fails, the grenade still winds up somewhere. Roll 1d6 to see how many spaces away from its intended target it lands. It’s up to the Narrator which direction it goes. This grenade throws out an irritating smoke that stings the eyes. The smoke lasts for six rounds. Anyone inside an affected space must make a Challenging Resilience check when they start aturnin or enter such a space or be blinded. They can make another Challenging Resilience check at the start of each of their following turns to recover from the effects. They remain blinded until they succeed.

narrativeItems.name: Death Stone
narrativeItems.description: The user knows the location of everyone who is about to die at any moment, along with the circumstances surrounding their death. They can teleport anywhere so that they can escort the dead to their final reward. The user can also instantly kill anyone with a touch. They cannot, however, spare the lives of the dying, no matter how much they might wish to.

narrativeItems.name: Mind Stone
narrativeItems.description: The user has incredible telepathic powers. They gain all powers from the Telekinesis and Telepathy power sets, ignoring any prerequisites. They can also travel through dreams and can construct psychic weapons. They also gain Brilliance 4 and Uncanny 4.

narrativeItems.name: Power Stone
narrativeItems.description: The user gains all powers from the Elemental Control (all types), Power Control and Super-Strength power sets, ignoring any prerequisites. They also gain Accuracy 4, Mighty 4 and Sturdy 4

narrativeItems.name: Reality Stone
narrativeItems.description: The user can alter physical reality as they see fit. They can change any physical characteristics like size, density or chemical composition. They gain all powers from the Elemental Control (all types), Luck, Phasing, Resize and Teleportation power sets, ignoring any prerequisites. They also gain Flight 2, Mighty 4 and Sturdy 4.

narrativeItems.name: Soul Stone
narrativeItems.description: The user can preserve souls for the afterlife, and they can examine a person's soul and even reach into a person's soul to damage it, heal it or alter its nature. They gain all powers from the Healing and Telepathy power sets, ignoring any prerequisites. They can also conjure soul-based constructs of other people, including super heroes and villains. They also gain Discipline 4 and Uncanny 4.

narrativeItems.name: Space Stone
narrativeItems.description: The user can travel in space and has control over the positions of things within it. This includes teleporting things of any size over unlimited distances. They gain all powers from the Teleportation power set, ignoring any prerequisites

narrativeItems.name: Time Stone
narrativeItems.description: The user can slow down, speed up and freeze time for everyone around them for themselves or for specific things. They can use this to age or youthen people or things as well. They can also see into the past and the future. They gain all powers from the Omniversal Travel (Time) and Sixth Sense power sets, ignoring any prerequisites

narrativeItems.name: Infinity Gauntlet
narrativeItems.description: The Infinity Gauntlet is a right-handed glove made of a golden metal, and it features a space for an Infinity Stone along the largest knuckles of each finger, plus a space for a sixth on the back of the hand. The user of the completed Infinity Gauntlet is omnipotent. They have the ability to remake all of reality in their universe in any way they see fit. They can destroy star systems at will-or kill half of all life in the universe with a snap of their fingers. The only limits to their power are their imagination-and, perhaps, their arrogance.

iconicVehicles.name: Aragorn
iconicVehicles.description: Although the steed of the Black Knight (Dane Whitman) is a living creature, he’s treated as a vehicle when carrying a rider. Whitman has sometimes aS entrusted Valkyries with Aragorn's care. Unfortunately, Aragorn died along with many of the Valkyries in the War of the Realms.

iconicVehicles.name: Captain America's Motorcycle
iconicVehicles.description: As Captain America, Steve Rogers has used a number of motorcycles over the years, even since his days in World War II. Most often, they are customized Harley-Davidsons.
notes: When riding a motorcycle, Captain America usually wears his shield on his back, offering him constant protection from that direction.
Another passenger can ride with Cap or in an attached sidecar.

teamManeuvers.name: Standard Offensive L1
teamManeuvers.description: The team members all get an edge on any attack they make this round.

teamManeuvers.name: Standard Offensive L2
teamManeuvers.description: The team members can each reroll all their dice on any attack they make this round. They get to use the better result.

teamManeuvers.name: Standard Offensive L3
teamManeuvers.description: The team members can each turn their Marvel die to a Fantastic success on any attack roll they make this round against targets of equal or higher rank.

teamManeuvers.name: Standard Defensive L1
teamManeuvers.description: The team members all have Damage Reduction 2 for this round.

teamManeuvers.name: Standard Defensive L2
teamManeuvers.description: The team members all have Damage Reduction 4 for this round.

teamManeuvers.name: Standard Defensive L3
teamManeuvers.description: The team members all have Damage Reduction 8 for this round.

teamManeuvers.name: Standard Rally L1
teamManeuvers.description: All actions taken against team members have trouble this round.

teamManeuvers.name: Standard Rally L2
teamManeuvers.description: Each member of the team can make a recovery roll for either Health or Focus, as if they had spent a point of Karma.

teamManeuvers.name: Standard Rally L3
teamManeuvers.description: A single member of the team who has been killed or shattered in this battle is healed to at least Health 0 and Focus 0.

teamManeuvers.name: Once an Avenger
teamManeuvers.description: The team can call on Captain America (Steve Rogers) to help them for the rest of this fight. He's just passing through on a more pressing mission and must leave immediately afterward. (This maneuver can be used only once per adventure.)

teamManeuvers.name: Reaction Speed
teamManeuvers.description: Each team member gets an additional reaction this turn.

teamManeuvers.name: Big Blue Marvel
teamManeuvers.description: The team can call on Blue Marvel to help them for the rest of this fight. He's just passing through on a more pressing mission and must leave immediately afterward. (This maneuver can be used only once per adventure.)

teamManeuvers.name: Calling in Big Backup
teamManeuvers.description: A Helicarrier appears in the sky and hovers over the battle. It fights on the heroes’ side and attacks each foe in the open with one of its weapons at the end of each round. (This maneuver can be used only once per adventure.)

teamManeuvers.name: Justice Like Lightning
teamManeuvers.description: Everyone on the team gains 1 point of Karma, even if they normally don't have any.

teamManeuvers.name: The Ultimate Betrayal
teamManeuvers.description: One of the team’s foes (of the Narrator’s choice) reveals that they are an impostor and switch sides to help the team for the rest of this fight. The impostor must leave immediately afterward to maintain their cover. (This maneuver can be used only once per adventure.)

teamManeuvers.name: A Way Out
teamManeuvers.description: Everyone on the team instantly teleports back to the team’s headquarters. (This maneuver can be used only once per adventure.)

teamManeuvers.name: Sky-Cycling!
teamManeuvers.description: The team summons a group of Sky-Cycles, one for each member of the team.

teamManeuvers.name: Battle Suit Shuffle
teamManeuvers.description: The team makes space for one of their teammates to summon and equip a battle suit from their headquarters. If the teammate is already wearing a battle suit, the new one replaces the original one. In any case, that team member cannot take any actions this round, and at the end of the round, they are wearing the new battle suit.
